FinOps, leaders, and managers Copilot Analytics reports What do ready-to-use adoption and impact reports show? Leaders and analysts Advanced Reporting What custom cohorts, comparisons, and business outcomes need deeper analysis? heading: 2.1 Pre-Configuration Discovery Questions
content: Before your IT team touches any configuration, you should be able to answer these: # Question Why It Matters 1 How many qualifying Viva Insights service plans are assigned (not merely purchased), and how many are Microsoft 365 Copilot? Microsoft's Environment Requirements article explicitly counts 30 Copilot plus 20 Viva Insights as 50 Viva service plans, while the Dashboard article says 50 Copilot or 50 Viva Insights. Verify assigned service plans and tenant availability; use 50 Copilot plus agent activity as the conservative Agent Dashboard prerequisite. 2 Is the Entra ID manager hierarchy populated for most users? Determines automatic vs. manual dashboard access. If >20% of users lack a Manager attribute, auto-identification and Manager Settings will be unreliable. 3 Do you have an HR data export available (department, function, location)? Required for group-level filtering and BU-level rollups. See Data Strategy . 4 Has anyone been assigned the AI Administrator role? Required to manage dashboard access and privacy settings 5 Do you have compliance/legal requirements to exclude specific users? Drives exclusion list configuration (5-day processing) 6 Has Copilot already been r...

heading: 2.2 Decision Framework
content: Do you need user-level license utilization data? → Start with Admin Center Reports Do you have 1+ assigned Copilot licences AND want ROI metrics? → Enable Copilot Dashboard (50+ for advanced features) Do leaders want ready-to-use targeted reports? → Copilot Analytics Reports (auto-available with Dashboard) Are you deploying agents (Copilot Studio, etc.)? → Validate and enable the Agent Dashboard (preview) Do you need to monitor Copilot Credits by service or organisational scope? → Use the Consumption Dashboard , then reconcile with official billing records Do you need before/after adoption analysis or custom segmentation? → Configure Advanced Analytics TIP: Progressive Implementation Most customers benefit from implementing this guide's framework progressively. Start with admin center reports, add the Copilot Dashboard and Analytics Reports, validate the Agent and Consumption dashboards where relevant, then add Advanced Reporting for custom analysis.

heading: 5.1 Copilot Assisted Hours & Value
content: The primary ROI metric. Composed of three categories: Category What's counted Assistance factor Meeting Summaries Duration of each meeting summarized by Copilot Actual meeting duration (variable) Search & Summaries Chat prompts, email/doc summaries, analysis 6 minutes per action Content Creation Draft email, Draft in Word, Create presentation 6 minutes per action Assisted Value = Assisted Hours × Hourly Rate (default $72/hour, configurable per session). Diagnostic coverage and causality Optional diagnostic data affects Copilot assisted hours, emails sent using Copilot, Teams meetings and meeting hours summarized or recapped, and Teams chats summarized. Required diagnostic data still supplies a subset, so disabled optional diagnostics can undercount these measures. Confirm the tenant's diagnostic-data policy and metric coverage before comparing groups or periods. Assisted Hours and Assisted Value are modeled estimates based on Microsoft's assumptions and a session-only hourly rate. They do not prove realized savings or that Copilot caused a business outcome. Use a documented baseline, stable diagnostic coverage, and business outcome data before making ROI or causal claims. Source...

heading: 5.2 Licensing — What You Get with Copilot
content: Every M365 Copilot licence automatically includes the Viva Insights service plan at no additional cost. That bundled plan contributes to Viva Insights eligibility, but each capability still has its own assigned-plan threshold, role, access, data, cloud, and preview requirements. A separate Viva Insights purchase is needed when the measured population includes employees who do not receive the bundled plan through Copilot.

heading: 5.3 Privacy by Design
content: No individual tracking: All dashboard data is aggregated at group level Minimum group size: Default 10 (configurable down to 5) Differential privacy: Automatically applied to protect individuals Exclusion lists: Remove specific users from all analytics

heading: 5.4 Known Metric Underreporting
content: Microsoft has acknowledged two data collection issues. No action needed — data auto-corrects after the affected windows. Affected Metrics Affected Period Email features (drafts, summaries) June 1, 2025 – February 18, 2026 Meeting features (summaries, recap) September 6 – November 3, 2025

heading: In Scope
content: Microsoft 365 Copilot applications: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, Loop, OneNote, Edge, Copilot Chat Six official capability areas: admin center reports, Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, Consumption Dashboard, Copilot Analytics reports, and Advanced Reporting License utilization, ROI measurement, sentiment analysis, agent adoption

heading: Out of Scope
content: Other Copilot products: Copilot for Sales, Copilot for Service, GitHub Copilot, Security Copilot Copilot Chat users without a Microsoft 365 Copilot licence: covered by the current Microsoft 365 admin center Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat usage report and, when enabled and available, the Copilot Dashboard's Copilot Chat view. Microsoft Graph's licensed Copilot usage endpoint remains licensed-user only; use the admin report or appropriate Purview audit source for unlicensed scenarios. Broader Viva Insights productivity metrics (meeting time, focus time) — see Collaboration Analysis Guide Current Copilot Chat reporting The Microsoft 365 admin center provides a dedicated usage report for Copilot Chat users without a Microsoft 365 Copilot licence, including exportable user activity. The Copilot Dashboard also provides a Copilot Chat licence view when reporting is enabled and available. These are different from licensed-user Microsoft Graph usage reporting and from Purview audit evidence. Source: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat usage report (Last updated 10 July 2026) and Microsoft 365 Copilot reporting options for admins (Last updated 16 September 2025).

Read this to decide your data approach. Then hand off to IT with the Data Onboarding Guide for step-by-step implementation.

heading: 1. Why Upload Org Data? (Entra ID vs. Org Data Upload)
content: A common question: "We already have Department, Manager, and Organisation in Entra ID — why do we need to upload a CSV?" Entra ID provides a baseline, but org data uploads unlock significantly more analytics capability: Capability Entra ID Only With Org Data Upload Department filter on dashboard Yes (Entra ID "Department" attribute) Yes (from CSV — can use different/cleaner values) Job Function / Job Discipline filter No Yes (via FunctionType / Microsoft_JobDiscipline ) Location filter No Yes (via Location attribute) Level / Seniority filter No Yes (via LevelDesignation ) Custom attributes (Cost Centre, Agency, BU, Region) No Yes (up to 105 custom attributes) Multi-agency / multi-BU partitioning Yes — if each agency has a distinct email domain, use the system-generated Domain attribute (no CSV needed) Yes — use a custom Agency attribute for maximum flexibility (works even when agencies share one domain) Before/after cohort segmentation in Power BI No segmentation Yes (segment by any uploaded attribute) Manager hierarchy accuracy Depends on Entra ID completeness Can supplement/override with CSV ManagerId Data freshness control Updated by directory team (often delayed) Updated by ...

heading: 2. What Breaks with an Incomplete Entra ID Hierarchy?
content: If the Entra ID Manager attribute is missing or inconsistent for many users: Feature Impact Auto CxO Identification Fewer leaders are auto-identified. Users without a clear reporting line are excluded from the "top 3 levels" calculation. Manager Settings (team-level data) Managers without a populated Manager attribute or without enough direct reports won't see team-level data. Filter by manager hierarchy (Publish Reports) Published reports with "Filter by manager hierarchy" will show incomplete data. Advanced Analytics cohort segmentation Manager-based grouping will be inaccurate. Recommendation: If more than 20% of users lack a Manager attribute, prioritise fixing directory data or supplement with an org data CSV upload that includes correct ManagerId values.

heading: 3. Source Precedence Is Surface- and Attribute-Specific
content: An upload changes the source for the attributes supplied to the apps you select; it does not universally or permanently replace Microsoft Entra ID across Microsoft 365. Surface / attribute Controlling behaviour Operational rule Microsoft 365 User Profile Microsoft Entra is the default source. Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 can be prioritised; when Entra is missing or incomplete, uploaded data can fill gaps. Keep Profile precedence at Entra unless there is an approved reason to override it. Copilot Dashboard and Viva Insights Uploaded data shared with these apps supplies the mapped dashboard and analysis attributes. If both Viva and Microsoft 365 uploads exist, Microsoft documents a merge with the more recent upload taking priority. Document the owner and source for every supplied attribute; verify values in Dashboard filters and Viva data quality after each change. Viva ManagerId and Organization Parallel ingestion can make Entra the default for either attribute, even when the upload also contains it. Other uploaded fields continue to come from CSV or connectors. Use Configure Entra connection when the directory owns these two attributes; allow about 24 hours for the chang...

heading: Supported correction and rollback paths
content: Update: Upload only affected users and all attributes you intend to change. A blank value preserves the existing value because new uploads merge with previous data. Delete a value: Use the documented type-specific marker: two single quotes ( '' ) in CSV for strings (three in Excel), -1 for integers, a nonexistent in-tenant address for email values, or 01-01-0001 for dates. Correct history: Use Historical import with a CSV and a File valid as of date. Microsoft documents historical updates for Viva Insights data only. Return ManagerId / Organization to Entra: Enable those attributes under Configure Entra connection , then delete uploaded optional attributes you no longer need. Migration failure: Stop further imports and open a Microsoft support request. Microsoft documents temporary rollback only for critical migration issues in special cases. Do Not Use a Dummy-Row Rollback Do not use a one-row "Replace All" file as a general removal or rollback technique. It can leave an artificial hierarchy and break filters, partitions, or reports. Use the supported update/delete markers, parallel Entra restoration, or Microsoft Support instead. Source: Import and manage your organizational d...

heading: 6. Determine the Current Tenant State
content: Official Microsoft Sources Conflict Manage settings for the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Viva Insights web app (20 March 2026) still calls Viva web-app upload the recommended path for Viva Insights customers. Use Microsoft Entra plus .csv files for parallel data uploads (visible date 30 June 2025; refreshed 7 July 2026) says adding and editing uploads is no longer supported there. Upload and maintain data through the Microsoft 365 admin center (5 December 2025) says migration occurs in tenant-specific waves announced in Message Center. Safest rule: Message Center and the actual tenant UI control the procedure. A calendar date or missing tile alone does not prove which path is supported.

heading: Current-state gate
content: Read the latest tenant-specific Message Center post for organizational-data migration. Open admin.microsoft.com → Setup → Migration and imports and record whether Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 and its add/edit controls are available. Open the Viva Insights web app and record whether organizational-data add/edit, delete, and Replace All controls are present. Do not assume all four move together. Review existing connections so a second source is not introduced unintentionally. If Message Center and the live UI agree, follow that path in the Data Onboarding Guide . If they conflict, or neither surface offers a supported add/edit path, stop . Capture the Message Center post ID/date, screenshots, roles, tenant cloud, and existing connection status; then escalate through Microsoft Support or the account team before uploading.

heading: Microsoft 365 Copilot Analytics
content: Multi-Agency Reporting Pattern Decision Evaluate two author-created patterns for a shared tenant THIS GUIDE'S AUTHOR-CREATED ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS Default Priority and Restricted Reporting (formerly labelled "Isolation" and retained in existing URLs) are names created by this guide. They are not Microsoft product, security, compliance, or licensing terms. Microsoft documents the component controls; it does not document either combination as an end-to-end security boundary. This guide offers two reporting patterns for a shared Microsoft 365 tenant: Default Priority makes an agency group the leader's expected starting scope while accepting broader access; Restricted Reporting combines partition-scoped analyst queries with separate controls for Dashboard, Agent, export, delegation, managers, privileged actors, and Power BI. Neither pattern creates cryptographic, tenant, or independently certified security isolation. Want the full background? Read why multi-agency analytics is hard → Supported Environment and Licensing Gate Check the exact cloud, assigned service plans, tenant UI, Message Center, and every required surface before choosing a path. heading: Microsoft 365 Copilot Analytics
content: Why Multi-Agency Analytics Is Hard The architectural challenge of scoping analytics across agencies in a shared tenant Document Property Value Section Explain Audience CIO, Solution Architect Version 4.1 Last validated 16 July 2026

heading: When This Applies
content: Multi-agency analytics is a challenge when your organisation meets all of the following conditions: Single M365 tenant — all agencies share one tenant Multiple independent agencies (or business units, divisions, departments) that operate semi-autonomously Each agency needs Copilot analytics scoped to their own population The fundamental tension: Microsoft 365 Copilot analytics has several separately governed surfaces. In a shared tenant, there is no single partition or product setting that scopes the Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, metrics export, delegation, manager access, analyst queries, privileged roles, and downstream Power BI sharing together. A user with a tenant-wide access source can see "Your company" data even when an agency analyst is assigned only to an agency partition. The patterns below coordinate those independent controls. They reduce accidental cross-agency reporting, but they do not create cryptographic, tenant, or product security isolation. Single-Agency Tenants If your tenant serves only one agency (or your agencies don't need separate analytics scoping), none of this applies. Follow the standard Admin Setup Guide instead.

heading: The Two Architectures
content: THIS GUIDE'S AUTHOR-CREATED ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS Default Priority and Restricted Reporting (formerly labelled "Isolation" in this guide and retained in existing URLs) are editorial names created by this guide. They are not Microsoft product, security, compliance, or licensing terms. Microsoft documents the component capabilities, including partitions; Microsoft does not document this guide's combined pattern as an end-to-end security boundary. Default Priority is the simpler author-created option. An agency leader's default dashboard scope can follow their Entra ID manager hierarchy when Microsoft's access and hierarchy conditions are met. The trade-off is intentional: a user with a tenant-wide access source may still use "Your company", and delegates inherit their delegator's scope. Choose this only when that visibility is accepted and documented. Setup typically takes 2–4 hours. Restricted Reporting is the more controlled author-created option. It combines group-scoped Viva feature access management (VFAM), partition-scoped analyst query workspaces, restricted global access, and dedicated Power BI delivery. A partition limits the rows and columns available to an assigned anal...

heading: Comparison at a Glance
content: Dimension Default Priority Restricted Reporting Core principle Agency CEOs see their team by default via manager hierarchy Partition-scoped analyst queries plus separate access and delivery controls Cross-agency visibility Possible — users can navigate to "Your company" Reduced by surface-specific controls; residual tenant-wide and privileged access remains Partitions needed No Yes — one per agency Report delivery Native Copilot Dashboard Power BI workspaces (recommended) PowerShell required No Yes (VFAM lockdown only; partitions are created via UI) Setup effort 2–4 hours 1–3 days Best for Organisations where cross-agency visibility is acceptable Organisations that accept defense-in-depth reporting segmentation after risk and compliance review

heading: Surface-by-Actor Boundary Matrix
content: Actor and surface What the pattern controls Residual risk / required control Agency leader or manager — Copilot Dashboard Default Priority uses manager scope; Restricted Reporting can target the agency-user group with web-app/VFAM restrictions. A shared executive or cross-agency manager hierarchy can span agencies. A tenant-wide access grant or eligible delegation can broaden scope. Test each role and account directly. Agency analyst — advanced query workspace The assigned partition limits available query rows and columns. Wrong filters, overlapping membership, multiple partition assignments, or a conflict-of-interest analyst can broaden query access. The global partition remains tenant-wide. Leader, delegate, or analyst — Agent Dashboard and metrics export Separate feature/access policies may restrict entry. Partitions do not scope these surfaces. Agent Dashboard and metrics export are previews with separate prerequisites and cloud availability; test them independently. Report viewer — Power BI Workspace/app audience, Viewer role, sharing settings, and optional row-level security govern report access. Power BI permissions are independent of Viva partitions. Resharing, Build per...

heading: Supported-Environment Gate
content: Stop Before Design If the Required Surface Is Unsupported As of the dated Microsoft sources below, the Agent Dashboard is preview and its feature-specific article uses the conservative prerequisite of at least 50 Microsoft 365 Copilot licences plus agent activity; a tenant with only 50 Viva Insights licences must validate availability. Agent Dashboard is unavailable in national and regional clouds. Copilot metrics export is preview, and Microsoft's supported-environments matrix says GCC does not support Agent Dashboard, Copilot metrics export, or Copilot Power BI reports. Base Viva plan support does not establish feature parity. Validate the exact cloud, tenant UI, licences/service plans, Message Center, and required surface before implementation; escalate a documented mismatch to Microsoft Support.

heading: Mandatory Go-Live Evidence Gate
content: Test a representative account for every actor: agency leader, cross-agency manager, shared executive, delegate, agency analyst, multi-partition analyst, global-partition analyst, report Viewer, Power BI Member/Contributor, AI Administrator, Insights Administrator, Insights Analyst, and Global Administrator. For each account, run negative tests against the Copilot Dashboard, Analytics Reports, Agent Dashboard, weekly/daily metrics export, advanced query workspace, query-result download, delegation, manager-published reports, Power BI workspace/app, report export, Analyze in Excel, and resharing. Capture dated evidence: tester identity/role, group and partition memberships, VFAM/access policy export, org-data source and quality snapshot, expected denial, observed result, screenshot or audit record, Power BI permissions, test owner, and remediation ticket. Publication and go-live are vetoed if any negative test fails or cannot be executed. Remove access or roll back the change, preserve evidence, and open Microsoft Support when documented behavior and tenant behavior differ. Obtain written Security, Compliance/Legal, Privacy/DPO, Viva Insights owner, and Power BI owner sign-off on ...

heading: Rollback and Change Control
content: Record the current policies, access lists, role assignments, partition filters/analysts, org-data source/quality, query assets, and Power BI permissions before change. Use a test cohort first. Treat enabling partitions as a high-impact tenant change; Microsoft documents Support involvement to turn partitions off, and deletion can remove partition configuration and associated assets. For org-data changes, use supported update/delete behavior rather than dummy rows. Define an owner, maintenance window, success criteria, failure criteria, restoration sequence, approvers, and Support trigger before execution.

heading: Microsoft's Four Recommended Copilot Survey Questions
content: All survey options use the same four questions. Include these verbatim to enable comparison with Microsoft's global benchmarks. Respondents answer on a 5-point Likert scale (Strongly Disagree → Strongly Agree). Question # Statement (use verbatim) 1 Using Copilot helps improve the quality of my work or output 2 Using Copilot helps me spend less mental effort on mundane or repetitive tasks 3 Using Copilot allows me to complete tasks faster 4 When using Copilot I am more productive

heading: Option A: Viva Glint
content: In the Viva Glint admin portal, create a new survey program using the Microsoft Copilot Impact Survey template. The template includes the four recommended questions pre-configured. Set the survey audience (all Copilot-licenced users, or specific groups). Set the survey cadence — Microsoft recommends recurring deployment to track trends. Launch the survey. Results flow automatically to the Dashboard's Sentiment section.

heading: Option B: Viva Pulse
content: In the Copilot Dashboard, navigate to the Impact page. Scroll to the Sentiment section. Select Start new Pulse survey . The Copilot Impact template opens with the four questions pre-loaded. Customise audience and add optional extra questions → Send. Results automatically appear in the Dashboard Sentiment section once responses are collected.

heading: Option C: CSV Upload
content: Run your Copilot survey using any tool. Include the four recommended questions using the exact wording above. Export results and convert to a 100-point scale: Strongly Disagree=0, Disagree=25, Neutral=50, Agree=75, Strongly Agree=100. Format the CSV with columns: User AAD ID/Email address , Question 1 , Question 2 , Question 3 , Question 4 . Leave cells blank for unanswered questions. Navigate to insights.cloud.microsoft → Survey data → Data connections → Manage data sources . Under M365 Copilot Impact Survey data upload , select Start . Enter optional survey name and date range → browse to CSV → Upload . Results appear in the Dashboard Sentiment section within 2 days .

heading: Option D: Aggregated Results (Admin Centre)
content: Run your Copilot survey and compute the % of users who Agreed or Strongly Agreed with each question. Navigate to admin.microsoft.com → Reports → Adoption Score . Select AI adoption score → View details . Under Assess Copilot sentiment for your org , select Record survey results . Enter the aggregated percentages for each question → Save. Priority Rule: If You Upload Both If you upload survey data via both CSV (.csv file) and aggregated results (Admin Centre), only the CSV results are shown on the Dashboard. The aggregated results only appear if you subsequently delete the CSV data. Step 11: Wait for Data Processing Initial data processing: up to 7 days after licences assigned Ongoing refresh: daily, 28-day rolling window, ~6-day delay from current date Newly licenced employees: additional up to 7-day delay before inclusion

heading: 6B. Organizational Messages for Copilot Adoption
content: Organizational Messages allow IT admins to send targeted adoption nudges directly to users, encouraging Copilot usage based on their actual behaviour rather than static group membership.

heading: What Are Organizational Messages?
content: Organizational Messages deliver short, contextual prompts to employees through multiple channels. Messages can promote Copilot features, share tips, or nudge inactive users to try specific capabilities.

heading: Delivery Channels
content: Channel Where It Appears Best For Email User's Outlook inbox High-confidence reach; auditable; series campaigns for sustained adoption Teams Popovers In-context within Teams Catching users where they already work; meeting and chat context Windows Taskbar Windows desktop notification area Broad surface for daily desktop users Windows Notifications Windows notification centre Less intrusive; visible when user checks notifications Windows Spotlight Lock screen and spotlight surface Passive awareness; reaches users during transition moments

heading: Usage-Based Targeting
content: The key capability for Copilot adoption is usage-based targeting , which uses dynamic, pre-defined usage segments to reach the right users: Segment Who It Targets Recommended Message Never used Licensed users who haven't tried Copilot Introduction and first-step guidance Tried once Users with minimal initial use Tips for getting more value; specific feature suggestions Occasional users Users who use Copilot sporadically Workflow-specific prompts; "Did you know?" features Regular users Active users with consistent patterns Advanced features; power-user tips

heading: How to Configure
content: Navigate to admin.microsoft.com → Settings → Org settings → Organizational messages . Select + New message and choose the Copilot adoption template (or create a custom message). Under Audience , select Usage-based targeting to choose a product usage segment. Select the delivery channel (email, Teams, Windows). Set the schedule — one-time or recurring series. Preview and publish . TIP: Pair with Dashboard Insights Use the Copilot Dashboard Adoption page to identify groups with low adoption, then create targeted Organizational Messages for those groups. After sending, monitor the Dashboard to see if adoption improves — this creates a closed-loop adoption enablement workflow. Analytics Connection Organizational Messages do not appear in the Copilot Dashboard. To track message delivery and open rates, use the Organizational Messages reporting within the M365 Admin Centre. To measure the impact of messages on adoption, compare Dashboard adoption trends before and after message campaigns.

heading: 7. Tier 5: Advanced Analytics Setup
content: Estimated Time: 4–8 hours active + query runtime + ongoing maintenance Advanced Analytics enables custom Power BI reports using Viva Insights person queries. This is for analysts who need before/after adoption comparison, custom cohort segmentation, and historical data beyond the 28-day rolling window. Step 1: Assign Insights Analyst Role (15 min + 1–2 hours propagation) Identify the analyst(s) who will manage reports In M365 Admin Centre: Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Add-on Plan → Insights Analyst Add users → Save Wait 1–2 hours for permissions to propagate Verify: analyst should see "Create analysis" button in Viva Insights web app left navigation Step 2: Configure Partition Access (15 min) A partition defines which employees an analyst can query. Most tenants have a default Global partition covering all employees. To assign an analyst to an existing partition: Navigate to https://insights.cloud.microsoft Go to Admin → Privacy settings → Partitions Select the partition (usually Global ) → click Edit Under the Analysts tab, select Add analysts Search for the Insights Analyst by name or email → select → Save The analyst can access this partition's data within 1–2 hours NOTE...

heading: 3. Upload Steps — M365 Admin Center (Primary Path)
content: Use this path when Message Center and the live Microsoft 365 admin center show that add/edit organizational-data imports are supported for the tenant.

heading: Step 1: Ensure Role Assignment
content: The person performing the upload must have the Organizational Data Source Administrator role assigned in Entra ID. This role manages Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 and is separate from the Insights Administrator role. Assign via: admin.microsoft.com → Users → Active Users → select user → Manage roles → search for "Organizational Data Source Administrator"

heading: Step 2: Prepare Your CSV File
content: Encoding: UTF-8 (with or without BOM) File size: Upload directly from local files up to 25 MB . For larger CSV files, upload through SharePoint; the documented SharePoint file limit is 2 GB . Attribute naming: Use Microsoft_* naming convention (e.g., Microsoft_PersonEmail , Microsoft_ManagerEmail , Microsoft_Organization ) No EffectiveDate column: The M365 Admin Center uses a date picker in the UI, not a CSV column No spaces in header names: Use underscores (e.g., Cost_Center not Cost Center ) One row per employee: Include every employee you want reflected in analytics Source: Import and manage your organizational data with a .csv file — Step 3 (Microsoft Learn, updated 2 April 2026).

heading: Step 3: Navigate to Data Connections
content: admin.microsoft.com → Setup → Migration and imports → Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 → Data Connections

heading: Step 4: Start Import
content: First upload: Select Get started Subsequent uploads: Select New import

heading: Step 5: Select Target Apps
content: Choose which M365 applications will receive the uploaded data: Viva Insights — required for Copilot Dashboard and Advanced Analytics Copilot Dashboard — enables org-level filters on the dashboard Microsoft 365 Profile — populates org data in user profiles You can select all that apply. Each app may have its own required attributes.

heading: Step 6: Upload File and Map Reserved Attributes
content: Upload your CSV file Map the reserved attributes: Microsoft_PersonEmail , Microsoft_ManagerEmail , Microsoft_Organization The system will auto-detect columns that match Microsoft_* naming

heading: Step 7: Review Custom Attributes and Configure Access
content: For each custom attribute, set the access level: Access Level Who Can See Use Case Public All selected apps and their users Department, Location, Job Function Confidential Only authorised analysts (Viva Insights only) HourlyRate, Salary Band, Performance Rating Custom Specific apps only Attributes shared with Profile but not Insights

heading: Step 8: Validate and Submit
content: Review the summary and submit Processing time: A few hours for small files, up to 3 days for large files (>100K rows) Monitor status in the Data Connections page: Validating → Processing → Complete

heading: 6. Post-Upload Verification Checklist
content: After every upload, complete this checklist to confirm data quality before relying on dashboards.

heading: Step 1: Check Import Status
content: Status Meaning Action Success All rows processed. No errors. Proceed to Step 2. Partial Success Most rows processed. Some rows had warnings (e.g., unmatched manager emails). Download import log (Step 2). Fix and re-upload problem rows. Failed File rejected. No data was imported. Check error details. Common causes: missing required columns, encoding issues, attribute name conflicts. Suspended Processing was halted (usually by system or admin). Check Data Connections for details. Re-submit if needed.

heading: Step 2: Download Import Log
content: Look for rows with unmatched Microsoft_ManagerEmail values. These indicate managers whose email addresses don't exist in the uploaded data or in Entra ID.

heading: Step 3: Download Manager Log
content: This log shows employees whose ManagerEmail doesn't resolve to an active user in Entra ID. These employees will appear as "orphaned" in the manager hierarchy.

heading: Step 4: Check Attribute Coverage
content: Navigate to the Data Quality tab. Use an organization-approved acceptance threshold; this guide uses >95% coverage for critical segmentation attributes as an operational target, not as a Microsoft product requirement. Investigate any material drop from the pre-change baseline, because missing values can exclude people from filtered views.

heading: Step 5: Check Manager Tree Count
content: The number of root nodes in the manager hierarchy should be small (typically 1–5, representing CEO/top executives). If you see hundreds or thousands, many ManagerEmail values are missing or incorrect.

heading: Step 6: Spot-Check Dashboard
content: Open the Copilot Dashboard and verify the Organisation filter shows your uploaded values (not just Entra defaults) Confirm new employees appear in the population count Check that Job Function, Location, and other optional filters are populated (if uploaded)

heading: Step 7: Approve Connector Data (If Applicable)
content: If using an HRIS connector (SAP SuccessFactors or Workday) with "Manually approve" enabled: you have a 48-hour review window to inspect the data before it goes live. If not approved within 48 hours, the data is automatically approved.

heading: Refresh Behaviour by Method
content: Method Refresh Behaviour Key Differences M365 Admin Center CSV Incremental merge Upload only affected users. Rows and values omitted from the new file remain unchanged; blank values also preserve the previously uploaded value. Use documented type-specific deletion markers when a value must be removed. Tenant-enabled Viva Insights CSV Full replacement Must re-upload all employees every time. Missing employees will lose their org data. HRIS connectors Automated (weekly/monthly) No manual uploads required. Connector syncs on schedule. Monitor connector health in Data Connections. API-based import Incremental or full (configurable) Controlled via automation. Supports programmatic incremental refresh.

heading: Common Maintenance Scenarios (M365 Admin Center CSV)
content: Scenario What to Upload New hire 1 row with the new employee's data (incremental merge adds them) Department change 1 row with updated Microsoft_Organization value (merge updates existing record) Employee left Update the authoritative employment-status/source record and the app-specific attributes required by your retention and DSR process. Do not use Layer=-1 as a universal user-deletion instruction. Historical correction Use the Historical import option in Data Connections to submit data for a past date

heading: Supported Update, Delete, and Historical-Correction Operations
content: Change Supported operation Update current values Upload only affected users and include each attribute to update. Blank or omitted values preserve existing data. Delete a string value Use two single quotes ( '' ) in CSV, or three single quotes in Excel. Delete an integer value Use -1 for that integer attribute. Delete an email value Use a nonexistent email address in the tenant, such as noname@contoso.com . Delete a date value Use 01-01-0001 . Correct historical Viva Insights data Start a CSV Historical import , select the File valid as of date, and upload only the impacted employees with corrected values. Historical update is documented for Viva Insights only. Source: Import and manage your organizational data with a .csv file — Update or make other changes and Make retroactive updates (Microsoft Learn, updated 2 April 2026).

heading: Recommended Cadence
content: CSV upload: Monthly (minimum). Set a recurring calendar reminder. HRIS connectors: Weekly or monthly (configured in connector settings) API-based import: As frequently as your automation runs (daily is possible) Do Not Use a Dummy-Row Rollback A one-row "Replace All" upload can create an artificial hierarchy and disrupt filters, partitions, or reports. Use a corrective upload, documented deletion markers, parallel Entra restoration, or Microsoft Support.

heading: Data-Quality Validation and Rollback Runbook
content: Phase Required action and evidence 1. Baseline and backup Preserve the exact source CSV, mapping, selected apps, access classifications, file-valid date, connection configuration, import ID, and pre-change population/coverage counts. Export current organizational data where the authorized app exposes export, and capture representative Profile and Dashboard values. 2. Preflight Confirm Message Center and tenant UI path, tenant cloud, active connections, required attributes, 25 MB local / 2 GB SharePoint route, valid UPNs, manager-chain integrity, no unexpected row-count change, no prohibited personal fields, and no attributes on which partitions or auto-refresh queries depend being removed. 3. Validate and approve Run import validation, review every error/warning and the attribute mapping tally, and use manual approval when the connection offers it. Do not approve unexplained drops in people, hierarchy roots, or critical-attribute coverage. 4. Success criteria Status reports that data is in use; processed and rejected counts reconcile to the source; no new critical data-quality warnings appear; sample Profile, Organization, Job function, and manager-scope values match the source ...

heading: Where to Check
content: Check Where What to Look For Upload / connector status M365 Admin Center → Data Connections Last import date, status (Success / Partial / Failed) Data freshness indicator Viva Insights web app → Admin → Data hub Freshness badge showing days since last successful upload Attribute quality scores M365 Admin Center → Data Quality tab Coverage percentage per attribute (>95% target) Dashboard spot-check Copilot Dashboard → filters Filter values match current org structure

heading: Signs of Stale Data
content: Symptom Likely Cause New employees are missing from dashboards Upload hasn't been refreshed since they were hired Only one leader appears in Group view Manager hierarchy data is outdated or incomplete Old department/location values still showing in filters Org changes not reflected in latest upload Employee counts don't match HR system totals Leavers not removed or new hires not added

heading: Recommended Monitoring Cadence
content: When What to Do After every upload Complete the Post-Upload Verification Checklist (Section 6) Weekly (if using connectors) Check connector health in Data Connections. Verify last sync date. Monthly (if using CSV) Confirm upload completion, review Message Center and Service Health, check whether connection status or preview availability changed, and rerun Profile/Dashboard/Viva regression spot-checks. Quarterly Full audit: compare uploaded population against the HR system of record, review attribute coverage and manager-tree health, verify source ownership/precedence, and test one supported correction and rollback path in a controlled manner. Sources: Tenant-specific migration communication is documented in Upload and maintain data through the Microsoft 365 admin center — Migration and timeline (Microsoft Learn, updated 5 December 2025). For current service incidents, use Microsoft 365 admin center Health > Service health before treating a regression as local misconfiguration.

heading: 9. Key Rules
content: Upload for every employee — include every person you want reflected in analytics, not just Copilot-licensed users No TimeZone column — TimeZone is system-generated from Outlook settings. Including it in your CSV causes a validation error. M365 Admin Center path: no EffectiveDate column — the date is selected via the UI date picker M365 Admin Center path: no spaces in header names — use underscores ( Cost_Center , not Cost Center ) Tenant-enabled Viva Insights add/edit path: include EffectiveDate — follow the date rule shown in the live workflow and current Microsoft guidance Incremental merge is the default in the M365 Admin Center — you only need to upload changed or new rows. Unmentioned employees retain their existing data. Data precedence is surface- and attribute-specific — Entra is the default for Microsoft 365 User Profile, while selected uploads supply mapped Copilot Dashboard/Viva attributes; parallel ingestion can return ManagerId and Organization to Entra. One import at a time per tenant — if an import is in progress, you must wait for it to complete before starting another

heading: 10. Common Upload Errors
content: Error Cause Fix Blank Microsoft_PersonEmail rows Rows with empty email addresses Remove blank rows or populate with valid UPNs. Validation fails if any required field is blank. Missing required attributes for selected apps Target app (e.g., Viva Insights) requires specific attributes that are not in the CSV Check which attributes each selected app requires and add them to the CSV. Empty data rows Rows with all-blank values (often trailing rows from Excel export) Remove empty rows from the CSV before upload. These block validation. Sending data to unlicensed apps Selected a target app that your tenant doesn't have licences for Deselect the unlicensed app in Step 5 or acquire the required licences. Custom attributes sent to unsupported apps Not all apps support custom attributes Review app compatibility for custom attributes. Viva Insights supports custom attributes; others may not. Attribute name conflicts with Microsoft_ reserved names Custom attribute uses a name that starts with Microsoft_ and conflicts with reserved attributes Rename the custom attribute to avoid the Microsoft_ prefix, or map it to the correct reserved attribute. heading: Microsoft 365 Copilot Analytics
content: Privacy Configuration Guide Privacy thresholds, exclusion lists, data residency & compliance controls Document Property Value Section Setup Audience IT Admin / Governance Version 4.1 Last validated 16 July 2026

heading: Choose the Action by Intended Outcome
content: Policy goal Use this exact action Expected user result Validation Keep sensitive typed prompt text away from external web search, while allowing an answer from permitted internal Microsoft 365 data Prevent Copilot from processing content > Performing Web Searches External web search is blocked as a grounding source. Copilot can still return an internally grounded response. Use a synthetic sensitive-information-type value. Confirm the response has no web grounding and can still use an accessible internal test document. Prevent Copilot from processing or answering a typed prompt that contains a selected sensitive information type Prevent Copilot from processing content > Processing prompts (preview) Copilot returns no answer and does not use the prompt for internal or web searches. First confirm the preview has reached the tenant. Test a benign prompt and a synthetic matching prompt in each required app; capture policy simulation/alert evidence and the user result. Prevent selected labeled files or emails from contributing content to a response Content contains > Sensitivity labels with Prevent Copilot from processing content Matching content is not processed for the response summ...

heading: Table of Contents
content: Privacy Thresholds Exclusion Lists Data Residency Microsoft Purview DLP for Copilot Prompts GDPR and Regional Compliance Data Retention Supported Environments What to Read Next

heading: 1. Privacy Thresholds
content: Privacy thresholds protect aggregated organization insights ; they do not turn every Viva Insights output into aggregate-only data. Insights Analysts can receive de-identified row-level employee records in advanced analysis, and uploaded organizational attributes can make a person inferable when combined. Setting Default Configurable Range Minimum group size 10 users 5–unlimited (lowest allowed = 5)

heading: How Thresholds Work
content: Data suppression: Results for groups below the threshold are suppressed — they show as blank or "insufficient data" rather than displaying potentially identifiable information. Dashboard and manager/leader insights: These surfaces use protected aggregates, minimum group size, distribution masking, and differential privacy where documented. Advanced analysis: An Insights Analyst can query de-identified individual-level organizational and Microsoft 365 metrics. The encrypted person identifier is not a guarantee of anonymity if title, location, organization, or custom attributes enable inference. Access governance: Treat analyst output as potentially personal data. Use least-privilege partitions, require role-specific privacy/security training, define an approved analysis purpose, and audit authored queries against policy and any data protection impact assessment. Sources: Advanced analysis privacy — Personal data, de-identified data, and calculated data (Microsoft Learn, updated 10 December 2025) documents de-identified row-level output and re-identification risk; Roles in Viva Insights — Levels of responsibility (7 July 2026) distinguishes analyst row-level access; and Advanced a...

heading: 2. Exclusion Lists
content: The unified exclusion list is a reporting control , not a universal statement that data is never collected or processed. It tells the documented Dashboard and Viva reporting surfaces not to show specified users in their aggregates.

heading: What Gets Excluded
content: Microsoft Copilot Dashboard and Agent Dashboard aggregated insights Viva Insights behavioral collaboration reports in advanced analysis Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat (web) usage in the Copilot Dashboard, including a Microsoft 365 licensed user who does not have a Copilot or Viva Insights license Microsoft Learn does not describe this list as deleting source data or excluding a person from every query type and processing path. For exclusion from advanced-analysis processing, manage the user's Viva Insights service plan as part of the data-subject-request workflow below.

heading: How to Upload
content: Method Steps CSV upload Prepare a CSV with user email addresses → Microsoft 365 admin center → Microsoft Viva → Viva Insights → Set up and management → unified exclusion list. Confirm the current labels in the tenant UI. Microsoft Entra groups Create or select an Entra group containing users to exclude, then configure the unified exclusion list through the current Microsoft 365 admin center workflow.

heading: Processing Times
content: Action Time to Take Effect Upload and complete an exclusion list Could take up to 5 days Documented removal change Microsoft separately states that a removal change can take up to 3 days ; verify the resulting membership and report behavior in the tenant. Plan Exclusions Before Dashboard Launch Because exclusion-list processing can take up to five days, configure and verify the list before launch. Confirm the policy is on, inspect the resulting membership, and test the affected reports after the processing window. Source: Manage settings for the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Viva Insights web app — unified exclusion list (Microsoft Learn, updated 20 March 2026).

heading: 3. Data Residency
content: Microsoft documents local data-at-rest behavior for Viva Insights advanced analysis, manager, and leader collaboration activities and metric computations . The location is determined by the tenant's default geography (default mailbox region), not by each user's location. It is not a universal statement about every Copilot Analytics workload. Viva Insights provisioned after Tenant default mailbox region Advanced, manager, and leader data stored at rest in 4 June 2024 Australia (AUS) Australia 12 August 2024 Japan (JPN) Japan 13 September 2024 Canada (CA) Canada 13 September 2024 United Kingdom (GB) United Kingdom Customers provisioned before the applicable date continue to be hosted in the region used before local residency became available. Use Microsoft's static workload-location table for that case rather than inferring location from the current tenant geography.

heading: Verification Steps
content: Record the tenant default mailbox region and the date Viva Insights was provisioned. Apply the matrix above. For earlier provisioning, consult the linked static workload-location information. If either value is unavailable or the result is material to a residency attestation, obtain tenant-specific confirmation through Microsoft Support; do not infer it from a user mailbox or a generic tenant-region label. Have legal/compliance approve the documented workload scope and evidence before making a sovereignty statement. Source: Viva Insights data residency for advanced analysis, managers, and leaders (Microsoft Learn, updated 23 June 2025), including its link to static data location information for select workloads .

heading: 3B. Microsoft Purview DLP for Copilot Prompts
content: Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention (DLP) uses the Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat policy location. The actions below have different effects and must not be described as one generic prompt block.

heading: Conditions, Actions, and Status
content: Condition and exact action Status Documented result Content contains > Sensitive information types Prevent Copilot from processing content > Performing Web Searches Supported action; the article does not label this action preview Blocks external web search as a grounding source. Copilot can still respond using permitted internal Microsoft 365 data. Content contains > Sensitive information types Prevent Copilot from processing content > Processing prompts Preview ; rolling out to tenants Copilot does not respond, and the prompt is not used for internal or web searches. Check whether rollout has reached the tenant. Applies to Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Chat, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and prebuilt agents. Content contains > Sensitivity labels Prevent Copilot from processing content Generally available Selected labeled files and emails are not processed or used in the response summary, although an item can still appear in citations. Calendar invites are unsupported; documented email coverage starts 1 January 2025. Prompt SIT Blocking Is Preview Do not treat Processing prompts as a guaranteed production boundary until the tenant exposes the preview control and a positive/negative ...

heading: Important Limitations
content: DLP evaluates text typed directly into a prompt; it cannot scan the contents of files uploaded directly in prompts. Sensitive-information-type and sensitivity-label conditions cannot be combined in the same rule, although separate rules can be placed in one policy. The location is available only in a Custom policy template; DLP alerts, notifications, and simulation mode are supported. Policy updates can take up to four hours to appear in Copilot experiences.

heading: Why This Matters for Analytics Teams
content: Analytics-count impact is not verifiable from Microsoft Learn. The DLP article documents response and search behavior, but does not state whether a blocked attempt is logged or counted in Copilot Analytics. Do not use DLP as an explanation for adoption gaps without a controlled tenant test or Microsoft Support confirmation that covers the specific report and metric. Coordinate Security/Compliance and Analytics teams so policy tests, dates, affected users, and observed report behavior are recorded together.

heading: Validate the Unknown Analytics-Count Impact
content: Microsoft Learn does not document whether a DLP-blocked attempt appears in each Copilot Analytics metric. Do not infer the answer from the DLP alert or from one dashboard total. Use this controlled protocol when the distinction is operationally material: Approve the test: use a non-production or approved pilot cohort, synthetic values only, one documented DLP policy/action, and a fixed UTC test window. Establish controls: from the same app and test identity, submit a benign prompt that should succeed and a synthetic matching prompt that should be restricted. Record exact timestamps and expected outcomes. Prove policy behavior: retain the DLP simulation/alert record and the observed Copilot response for both prompts. This proves enforcement, not analytics counting. Wait for each report's documented latency: inspect the relevant Copilot Dashboard metric, admin-center report, export, or advanced query using the same population and time boundary. Do not compare different report windows. Record the result as tenant-specific: document whether the successful and blocked attempts can be distinguished, the report/metric/version, policy action, latency, and any ambiguity. Escalate ambigui...

heading: Configuration Reference
content: DLP for Copilot is configured in the Microsoft Purview portal , not in Viva Insights. Source: Microsoft Purview DLP for Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat (Microsoft Learn, updated 10 June 2026). Complementary to Analytics DLP for Copilot and Copilot Analytics serve different purposes but are part of the same deployment posture. A well-governed Copilot deployment uses DLP to prevent sensitive data exposure, Copilot Analytics to measure adoption and impact, and Organisational Messages to drive enablement. Consider establishing a cross-functional team that includes Security (Purview), IT (Admin Centre), and Analytics (Viva Insights) stakeholders.

heading: 4. GDPR and Regional Compliance
content: Microsoft 365 Copilot Analytics is designed with GDPR compliance in mind. The following controls support regulatory requirements: End-user opt-out: When enabled, this removes the user's behavioral metrics from future row-level outputs. It does not apply to aggregated organization insights, meeting-query results, or row-level Copilot usage data. Data minimisation: Dashboards and manager/leader insights use protected aggregates; advanced-analysis queries can contain de-identified individual rows. Limit attributes, rows, retention, and query purpose to what the approved analysis requires. Org data handling: If you upload organisational data (CSV with attributes like Department, Agency, Location), ensure the upload process follows your organisation's data handling policies. Do not include sensitive personal data in org data attributes.

heading: Data Subject Request Control Path
content: Request Accountable action Validate and own the request The organization is the data controller. Legal/privacy evaluates validity, scope, identity, jurisdiction, and required response; Microsoft acts as data processor. Exclude from advanced-analysis processing Do not assign, or remove, the user's Viva Insights service plan. Microsoft notes that removing the service plan removes the person and collaboration data from the measured population for advanced analysis and organization insights, but does not remove the person from the Copilot Dashboard measured population. Exclude from Copilot Dashboard aggregates Use and verify the unified exclusion list described in Section 2. Access Use the Viva Insights export mechanism for raw, personally associable data within the validated request scope; aggregate metrics from which no personal data can be gleaned are outside that export scope. Correction Correct the source system: Microsoft 365 email/calendar data or the uploaded organizational-data source. Viva Insights does not correct source data itself. Deletion Use the Microsoft 365 GDPR Right to erasure process as applicable; delete reports that identify the subject and remove the subject ...

heading: 5. Data Retention
content: Retention of backend Viva Insights data is different from a dashboard display window or an export range. Do not use one as proof of the other. Data Source Retention Period Notes Advanced Analytics — Collaboration metrics Initially 13 months ; grows weekly to a maximum of 27 months No collaboration data older than 27 months is retained. Advanced Analytics — Copilot metrics Maximum 13 months Microsoft 365 Copilot metrics do not grow to the 27-month collaboration window. Dashboard views, dashboard export, and Microsoft 365 admin usage reports Separate feature-specific windows Use the current documentation for the specific surface; these windows do not redefine the 27/13-month Viva Insights retention policy. Govern Long-Term Snapshots Separately If you retain exported or Power BI snapshots beyond a product's interactive window, apply an approved business purpose, access controls, retention schedule, and deletion process. A local snapshot is a new governed copy. Source: Advanced analysis privacy — Retaining sensitive data (Microsoft Learn, updated 10 December 2025).

content: Feature Worldwide GCC Manage privacy settings Yes Yes Unified exclusion list for Copilot Dashboard Yes Yes Custom advanced-analysis queries Yes Yes Copilot Power BI reports Yes No Behavioral Power BI reports Yes Only Business resilience, Impact explorer, Meeting effectiveness, Recurring meeting audit, Ways of working, and Wellbeing – Balance and flexibility Agent Dashboard / Copilot metrics export Public Preview No The reviewed feature matrix covers Worldwide and GCC. Do not infer GCC High or DoD feature parity from plan-level support; validate the exact cloud, Message Center, and tenant UI before committing to a control or report. Source: Supported Microsoft 365 environments for Viva Insights features (Microsoft Learn, updated 30 June 2026).

heading: 1. Default Priority Pattern Setup
content: Estimated time: 2–4 hours  |  PowerShell required: No This author-created pattern relies on native Copilot Dashboard scoping ("Your group") driven by Entra ID manager hierarchy. An agency CEO can receive that scope only when access, tenant, and reporting-line conditions are met (see callout below). Cross-agency and tenant-wide visibility from other access sources is explicitly accepted in this model. Automatic Access Has Hard Prerequisites Per Microsoft Learn , automatic dashboard access for senior leaders is granted only when both tenant criteria are met: The tenant has at least 2,500 users with an Entra ID; and The majority of users share a single reporting line (e.g., report up to the same CEO). Within a qualifying tenant, a leader is auto-identified only if they are in the top three levels with a significant portion of the org reporting to them. A separate agency's top leader is auto-identified only if that agency's hierarchy tree is at least 4% of the overall company size . Consequences for multi-agency tenants: Tenant under 2,500 users → no agency CEO gets automatic access. Use manual access grants + delegation (Phase 2). Small agency (<4% of the tenant) → its CE...

content: Complete all five steps in this guide's implementation framework in the Admin Setup Guide before proceeding. The five-step sequence is an author-created implementation aid, not Microsoft's capability taxonomy. heading: Phase 2: Grant Additional Access
content: Step Action Where Expected Result 2.1 Delegate to agency CIOs / senior leaders Each agency CEO uses the Delegation feature (Copilot Dashboard → Settings → Delegation) to grant access to their CIO or other leaders Delegated leaders see the same "Your group" scope as the delegating CEO. 2.2 Grant Central IT "Your company" scope A senior leader who already holds "Your company" scope delegates to the Central IT lead via Copilot Dashboard → Settings → Delegation (delegation conveys the delegator's scope). If no qualifying senior leader exists, add the Central IT lead through Manage access to the Viva Insights web app (admin access list). Central IT lead sees "Your company" scope with tenant-wide data for cross-agency oversight.

heading: Phase 2.3: Enrich Org Data (Optional)
content: If the Entra ID connection provides sufficient attributes ( ManagerId , Organization ), no additional data upload is needed. If you require custom attributes (e.g., Agency , FunctionType , CostCentre ), upload a CSV supplement: Enable the Entra connection for ManagerId + Organization attributes (if not already done in Phase 0). If custom attributes are needed, prepare a CSV file following the CSV Templates (Section 4). Upload via the steps in the Data Onboarding Guide .

heading: Phase 3: Create Partitions (Optional)
content: Partitions are optional in the Default Priority pattern. Use them when you want the rows and columns available in an analyst query workspace scoped to an agency; they do not scope the other surfaces listed in the boundary matrix . Navigate to Viva Insights web app → Admin → Privacy settings → Partitions . Click New partition . Name the partition after the agency (e.g., "Agency A"). Set the filter to the relevant attribute (e.g., Organization = "Agency A" or Agency = "Agency A" ). Assign the partition's analyst(s). Repeat for each agency.

heading: Phase 4: Power BI Reports (Optional)
content: For deeper analysis beyond the native dashboard, analysts can create Power BI reports from partition-scoped queries. The assigned analyst opens their partition in the Viva Insights web app. Creates a Copilot person query (or uses a pre-built template). Connects the query output to Power BI Desktop. Publishes the report to a Power BI workspace dedicated to the agency. Shares the workspace with agency leadership. For step-by-step guidance on publishing to the Power BI service and assigning stakeholders by workspace role (Viewer, Member, etc.) — plus the per-viewer licensing implications — see Analyst Playbook — Share via Power BI (§5.2) . Default Priority Setup Complete Configuration is complete only after the expected leader and delegate scopes, optional analyst query workspaces, and Power BI permissions pass the negative-test and evidence gate in Section 0 . Continue to Ongoing Governance .

heading: 2. Restricted Reporting Pattern Setup
content: Estimated time: 1–3 days  |  PowerShell required: Yes (group-scoped VFAM restrictions; partitions are created via the Viva Insights web app UI) This author-created pattern combines group-scoped dashboard restrictions, partition-scoped analyst query workspaces, controlled privileged access, and dedicated Power BI delivery. A partition limits rows and columns in its assigned analyst workspace; it does not guarantee what that person can access through Dashboard, Agent, export, delegation, manager, global, administrative, downloaded-result, or Power BI surfaces. The design is defense in depth and reporting segmentation, not tenant or security isolation.

heading: Phase 0: Prerequisites
content: Complete through Tier 2, Step 3 in the Admin Setup Guide . This establishes: Copilot and Viva Insights service-plan assignment using the conflict-safe licensing wording in Section 0 Insights Admin and Insights Analyst roles VFAM verification that the dashboard toggle and Advanced Analytics toggle are accessible Do NOT Enable Broad Access Stop after verifying VFAM in Tier 2, Step 3. Do not enable AutoCxO, delegation, or broad dashboard access for "Everyone". Restricted Reporting applies group-scoped controls in Phase 1 and then verifies each surface independently.

heading: Phase 1: Group-Scoped VFAM Restrictions
content: Target direct Viva Insights web-app and Copilot Dashboard access to the approved Central IT population, while agency leaders receive reports through separately governed Power BI delivery. This is one layer of the pattern and must be tested alongside all other surfaces. Important: Dashboard Access Is Now Managed via the Viva Insights Web App Toggle Per Manage settings for the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Viva Insights web app (last updated 20 March 2026), the target state uses the Viva Insights web app control; pre-rollout tenants may still display the legacy Copilot Dashboard name, and existing policies carry forward. Disabling the web-app control removes the targeted user's access to the dashboard, Copilot Analytics Reports, Agent Dashboard, and advanced analyst tools. Confirm the actual tenant label and effective policy before relying on it. Step Action Details 1.1 Disable Auto CxO Identification Viva Insights web app → Admin → General → Auto CxO Identification → toggle Off (or run the AutoCxoIdentification VFAM disable policy) 1.2 Disable Delegation Viva Insights web app → Admin → Delegation → toggle Off (or run the CopilotDashboardDelegation VFAM disable...

heading: Phase 2: Data Strategy
content: Choose your data path based on whether your agencies have distinct email domains or you need a custom attribute for agency identification.

heading: Choose Your Path
content: Criterion Path A — CSV with Agency Attribute Path B — Entra Domain Filter (Zero CSV) When to use Agencies share a common email domain, or you need custom grouping that doesn't align with email domains Each agency uses a distinct email domain (e.g., @agencyA.gov , @agencyB.gov ) Data upload CSV with Agency attribute (see Section 4 ) No upload needed — Domain is a system-generated attribute from email addresses Partition filter Filter by Agency attribute Filter by Domain attribute Maintenance Re-upload CSV monthly or on org change Zero maintenance — domain data is automatic

heading: Path A: CSV with Agency Attribute
content: Prepare a CSV file with the following columns (see Section 4 for a template): Microsoft_PersonEmail (required) Microsoft_ManagerEmail (required) Microsoft_Organization (required) EffectiveDate (required) Agency (custom attribute — this is the partition filter) Follow the upload steps in the Data Onboarding Guide . After upload, verify the Agency attribute appears in Viva Insights → Admin → Data quality.

heading: Path B: Entra Domain Filter (Zero CSV)
content: No data upload is required. Verify each agency has a distinct email domain in Entra ID (e.g., @agencyA.gov , @agencyB.gov ). The Domain attribute is system-generated from user email addresses and is automatically available for partition filtering.

heading: Phase 3: Create Partitions
content: Create one partition per agency. Optionally, create a global partition for CIO-level cross-agency reporting. Step Action Details 3.1 Navigate to partitions Viva Insights web app → Admin → Privacy settings → Partitions 3.2 Create agency partition Click New partition . Name it after the agency (e.g., "Agency A"). Set the filter: Path A: Agency = "Agency A" Path B: Domain = "agencyA.gov" 3.3 Assign analyst(s) Assign the per-agency analyst to the partition. In that partition's analyst query workspace, available rows and columns are scoped by the partition filter. Verify the analyst has no unintended global or multi-partition assignment and test their other access surfaces separately. 3.4 Repeat for each agency Create separate partitions for Agency B, Agency C, etc., each with its own filter and analyst(s). 3.5 Create global partition (optional) For CIO-level reporting, create a partition with no filter (includes all users). Assign the Central IT analyst.

heading: Phase 4: Report Delivery
content: In Restricted Reporting, the recommended agency-facing delivery path uses Power BI workspaces/apps rather than direct Copilot Dashboard access. Power BI permissions are a separate control plane and require their own least-privilege design and negative tests. Each per-agency analyst opens their partition in the Viva Insights web app. Creates a Copilot person query (or uses a pre-built template such as the M365 Copilot Adoption report). Connects the query output to Power BI Desktop. Publishes the report to a dedicated Power BI workspace for the agency (e.g., "Agency A — Copilot Analytics"). Shares the workspace with agency leadership (CEO, CIO, designated leaders). Repeat for each agency. For the detailed publish-and-assign steps (Power BI service, workspace roles such as Viewer, Entra-group access, and per-viewer Pro/PPU/Premium licensing), see Analyst Playbook — Share via Power BI (§5.2) . WARNING: Manager Publishing Can Broaden Access "Publish Reports" with a manager hierarchy filter can make enabled managers eligible for additional Viva Insights experiences. A shared executive or cross-agency manager hierarchy may span agencies. Use dedicated Power BI workspaces/apps as t...

content: Use this format when agencies share a common email domain and you need a custom Agency attribute for partition filtering. Microsoft_PersonEmail,Microsoft_ManagerEmail,Microsoft_Organization,EffectiveDate,Agency,FunctionType alice@contoso.gov,bob@contoso.gov,Finance,2026-04-01,Agency A,Corporate bob@contoso.gov,carol@contoso.gov,Finance,2026-04-01,Agency A,Leadership dave@contoso.gov,eve@contoso.gov,Engineering,2026-04-01,Agency B,Technical eve@contoso.gov,frank@contoso.gov,Engineering,2026-04-01,Agency B,Leadership grace@contoso.gov,frank@contoso.gov,Operations,2026-04-01,Agency C,Operations Column Required Description Microsoft_PersonEmail Yes Employee's primary email address (must match Entra ID UPN) Microsoft_ManagerEmail Yes Direct manager's email address (builds the reporting chain) Microsoft_Organization Yes Department or business unit name EffectiveDate Yes Date the record takes effect (ISO 8601: YYYY-MM-DD ) Agency Custom Agency identifier used as partition filter FunctionType Custom Optional — functional classification for deeper analysis
