heading: 1. Copilot Dashboard Pages
content: The Copilot Dashboard is accessible at insights.cloud.microsoft and is divided into three main pages: Readiness , Adoption , and Impact (which includes a Sentiment section). Each page answers a different question about your organisation's Copilot deployment.

heading: 1.1 Readiness Page
content: The Readiness page answers: "How many licences have we assigned, and is the count growing?" Licence assignment status — current count of assigned vs available M365 Copilot licences. Trend chart — historical view of licence assignments over time, so you can spot stalled rollouts. Feature Availability by Licence Count Tenants with 1+ assigned Copilot licences get core Readiness, Adoption, and Impact capabilities. For capabilities using the 50-plan threshold, Microsoft's Environment requirements for Viva Insights (Last updated 12 May 2026) explicitly counts the Viva Insights service plan included with Copilot and gives 30 Copilot + 20 Viva Insights = 50 qualifying service plans . The newer, Dashboard-specific Connect to the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard for Microsoft 365 customers (Last updated 1 July 2026) instead says 50 Copilot or 50 Viva Insights licences. Do not assume a mixed deployment fails: verify assigned service plans, feature presence, and processing in the tenant; escalate to Microsoft Support if the documented qualifying mix does not unlock the capability. For Agent Dashboard (preview) , conservatively require 50 assigned Microsoft 365 Copilot licences plus qualifying a...

heading: 1.2 Adoption Page
content: The Adoption page answers: "Who is using Copilot, how often, and in which apps?"

heading: Key Metrics
content: Metric Definition Active Copilot users Number of licensed users who had at least one Copilot interaction in the 28-day measurement window. Copilot Actions Taken Total count of Copilot interactions across all users. Excludes agent interactions — those are tracked on the Agent Dashboard. Usage Intensity Average number of actions per active user (Actions Taken ÷ Active Users). Measures depth of engagement beyond simple headcount. Consistent Usage Users who were active in both the current and the previous 28-day period. Shows sustained adoption rather than one-time trial. Internal Benchmark Your own historical trend line — compare current period to your past performance. External Benchmark Anonymised comparison against similar-size organisations. Helps answer "Are we ahead or behind?"

heading: Per-App Breakdown
content: The Adoption page breaks down usage across individual M365 applications: App What It Tracks Teams Meeting summaries, chat Copilot interactions, Teams Copilot actions Outlook Email drafting, summarisation, coaching suggestions Word Document drafting, rewriting, summarisation PowerPoint Slide generation, design suggestions, narrative creation Excel Formula generation, data analysis, chart creation Copilot Chat Current merged Copilot Chat usage metric across the supported Chat experience Copilot Chat (web) — legacy Historical transition series only; expect it to trend toward zero as activity moves into the merged Copilot Chat metric License Filter: Microsoft 365 Copilot vs Copilot Chat At the top of the Adoption page, a License filter lets you compare Microsoft 365 Copilot , Copilot Chat , and, where the 50-plan experience is available, All . The Copilot Chat view covers active users without the paid Microsoft 365 Copilot add-on when reporting is enabled. Merged metric and legacy-web transition Use Copilot Chat as the current reporting metric. Do not interpret a decline in the legacy Copilot Chat (web) series as declining adoption by itself; Microsoft is merging current activity in...

heading: Intent Patterns (Preview)
content: The Adoption page includes intent-based metrics that categorise supported Copilot Chat prompts into common intent categories. These help you understand how people use Copilot, not just how much . Intent Category What It Captures Example Prompts Ask and find Questions and information retrieval "Find the recording of meeting X from May", "Who is the project lead for initiative Y?" Catch up Summaries, recaps, and action items "Recap this meeting with key takeaways", "Summarise what happened while I was out" Draft and brainstorm Content generation and creative ideation "Draft a status update email about project X", "Brainstorm creative presentation ideas" Other Prompts that don't fit the above categories General questions, casual interactions Intent metrics are also available in Viva Insights advanced reporting for deeper analysis.

heading: Expanded App Coverage
content: Beyond the core M365 apps listed above, the Dashboard also tracks Copilot usage in additional surfaces. Scenario-level breakdowns (e.g., suggested reply, translate, coach, clean data) are available for some apps: App/Surface Scenarios Tracked Microsoft 365 Copilot App Copilot Chat prompts and interactions Edge Copilot Chat interactions in the browser sidebar OneNote Summarise, organise, and create content from notes Outlook Suggested reply, translate, coach (in addition to draft/summarise) PowerPoint Translate, designer visuals, suggestions (in addition to create/summarise) Excel Clean data, Python in Excel (in addition to formula/analysis/format) Intent Metrics Are in Public Preview Intent-based metrics are rolling out in public preview as of April 2026. Availability may vary by tenant. These metrics will also be available in Viva Insights custom person queries once generally available, enabling analysts to correlate intent patterns with organisational attributes.

heading: "View by" Options
content: The Adoption page can be sliced by different organisational dimensions. Each requires specific data to be present: View By What It Shows Data Requirement Group Adoption by manager's direct/indirect reports Requires manager hierarchy (ManagerId in org data CSV or Entra's Manager field) Job Function Adoption by role category (Engineering, Sales, HR, etc.) Requires FunctionType attribute in org data Organization Adoption by business unit or department Requires Organization attribute in org data or Entra Department field License type Adoption by Microsoft 365 Copilot vs Copilot Chat Copilot Chat filter requires tenant-level Copilot Chat reporting to be enabled "View by: Group" Shows Only One Person? If a leader selects "View by: Group" and sees only themselves (or far fewer people than expected), the most common cause is missing intermediate managers in the org data . The manager hierarchy chain must include a row for every manager between the leader and their reports. Verify that your CSV (or Entra Manager field) has rows for every intermediate manager. Gaps break the chain and orphan the downstream employees from the leader's view.

heading: Scope Filter
content: Scope What It Shows Who Gets It Your company Entire eligible tenant population Global Administrator; qualified senior leader; explicitly assigned company-wide user; Insights Analyst assigned to the Global partition; or eligible company-scoped delegate. AI Administrator and Insights Administrator roles alone do not grant this view. <Leader Name> That leader's reporting chain (direct + indirect reports) Leaders with enabled Copilot Dashboard access whose hierarchy is in org data Your group Your own eligible direct and indirect reports Manager enabled through Manager Settings and meeting licence, hierarchy, and minimum-group requirements; or a group-scoped delegate Sources: Roles in Viva Insights (Last updated 7 July 2026), Delegate access to the Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Copilot insights (Last updated 9 February 2026), and Configure manager settings (Last updated 25 June 2026).

heading: Suppressed Values ("--")
content: When you see "--" instead of a number, it means the group size is below the minimum group size threshold (default: 10). This is a privacy protection , not an error. Data is present but suppressed to prevent identification of individuals. If you need to see data for smaller teams, an Insights Admin can adjust the minimum group size in privacy settings — but this should be done carefully with HR and legal approval.

heading: Agent-Related Insight Cards on the Adoption Page (Preview)
content: In addition to the core Adoption insights (overview, usage intensity, returning users, per-app breakdown), the Adoption page now surfaces two agent-focused insight cards when your tenant has agent activity. These are independent of the Agent Dashboard itself and are visible to anyone who can already view the Adoption page. Card What It Shows Requires Agent adoption by Copilot users (preview) The number of Copilot users who have used at least one agent, the number of agents with at least one active Copilot user, and the top three agents sorted by active users (with change-over-time and agent-response counts). Lets you jump directly into the agent deep-dive page from the Adoption page. At least one active agent user in the tenant. No PAYG required. Credit usage overview Copilot Credits consumed by Copilot Chat users, the number of agents those credits were used across, and the number of Copilot Chat users driving credit consumption. Also breaks out the top groups driving credit usage (by group / organisation / job function) with 28-day change. Your tenant must have Pay-As-You-Go enabled for Copilot Studio / Copilot Chat. Without PAYG the card does not appear. Why these cards matte...

heading: 1.3 Impact Page
content: The Impact page answers: "What value is Copilot delivering?" Check diagnostic coverage before interpreting impact Optional diagnostic data affects Copilot assisted hours , emails sent using Copilot , Teams meetings summarized or recapped , meeting hours summarized or recapped , and Teams chats summarized . Required diagnostic data still provides a subset, so the metrics may populate while remaining materially undercounted. Record the tenant's optional diagnostic-data policy and keep it stable across comparison periods. Source: Connect to the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard for Microsoft 365 customers (Last updated 1 July 2026), Impact section.

heading: Top-Level Metrics
content: Metric Definition How It's Calculated Copilot Actions Taken Total Copilot interactions in the period Direct count of all Copilot actions (same as Adoption page) Copilot Assisted Hours Estimated hours saved by Copilot Meeting summaries + search/summaries valued at 6 min each + content creation valued at 6 min each Copilot Assisted Value Dollar value of time saved Assisted Hours × hourly rate (default $72/hr ). Hourly rate is session-only — changes are not saved between visits. Satisfaction Rate User satisfaction with Copilot responses Thumbs up ÷ (thumbs up + thumbs down) reactions, 28-day rolling window. No survey required — based on in-app feedback. Estimate, not causal proof Assisted Hours and Assisted Value are modeled estimates using Microsoft's assumptions and the hourly rate entered for the current session. They are not realized cash savings and do not establish that Copilot caused an outcome. For decision-grade analysis, document the assumptions, use a stable baseline and diagnostic-data policy, compare suitable cohorts or periods, and pair usage measures with independently governed business outcome data. Current documented metric issue and incident validation The current...

heading: Export Copilot Metrics Safely (Preview)
content: The Copilot metrics export is a preview feature with a fixed, non-customizable schema. It exports anonymized user-level metric rows, not the aggregated chart currently visible on screen.

heading: Prerequisites
content: The tenant must meet the documented 50-licence threshold. Because Microsoft pages conflict on mixed service-plan counting, verify assigned service plans and export availability in the tenant. The exporter must have global / Your company access . A manager or "Your group" delegate cannot export. The Copilot Metrics Export feature must be enabled for the user, and the capability must be available in the tenant's cloud. It is not available in GCC.

heading: How to Export
content: Open the Copilot Dashboard in the Viva Insights web app. Select the Copilot metrics Export control. Choose Week for weekly rows covering the past six months or Day for daily rows covering the past 28 days. Download the CSV and record the export date, granularity, source article date, and tenant/cloud used.

heading: What's in the Export
content: Property Correct interpretation Grain and period One anonymized user-level record per time interval: up to six months for Week, or 28 days for Day. Identity De-identified user identifiers are included. The export is therefore anonymized user-level data, not aggregate-only data. Customization The schema and metric selection are not customizable, and Dashboard filters/groupings do not redefine the export. Metrics The documented export metrics are provided in the fixed schema. Copilot Assisted Hours is not included.

heading: Privacy Handling
content: Treat the CSV as sensitive analytics data even though direct identities are removed; repeated user-level rows and joins with organizational data can increase re-identification risk. Store it in an access-controlled location, apply retention and sensitivity requirements, restrict downstream joins, and document every recipient and purpose. Do not present row-level data or attempt re-identification. Aggregate under approved minimum-group and privacy rules before wider distribution. Validate row counts, dates, granularity, metric definitions, missing values, and diagnostic-data coverage before publishing trends. Export vs Advanced Analytics The preview export provides a fixed anonymized user-level dataset. Use Advanced Reporting when an Insights Analyst needs governed metric selection, partition-scoped queries, or custom segmentation. Neither route removes the need for privacy review. Source: Export Copilot metrics from the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard (preview) (Last updated 15 July 2026).

heading: 1.4 Sentiment Section
content: The Sentiment section lives within the Impact page and shows qualitative user feedback about Copilot. Component What It Shows Notes Sentiment results by question Aggregated responses to Copilot experience questions Includes benchmark comparison against similar orgs Sentiment by group Heat map showing sentiment across organisational groups Viva Insights customers only — requires Viva Insights licence Manager team view Sentiment breakdown for a manager's direct reports Subject to minimum group size suppression No Survey Data? If no Copilot survey has been deployed, the Sentiment section will show "No survey responses available." This is expected. The Satisfaction Rate metric (thumbs up/down) works independently of surveys and will still display on the Impact page regardless of whether surveys have been configured.

heading: 2. How to Delegate Access
content: Dashboard access can be shared with others through delegation. This is how leaders give their managers or analysts visibility into Copilot usage for their scope.

heading: Two Methods to Add Delegates
content: Method Steps Method 1: Settings Dashboard → Settings (gear icon) → Delegation → Add → search for user → select scope → Save Method 2: Share Dashboard → Share button (top right) → Add → search for user → select scope → Save

heading: Delegation Rules
content: Rule Detail Maximum delegates 75 per delegator No cascading Delegation does not flow downward. If Alice delegates to Bob, Bob cannot re-delegate to Carol. "Your group" scope Delegate sees the delegator's reporting chain only "Your company" scope Delegate sees the entire tenant — use sparingly Agent Dashboard access Requires "Your company" scope. "Your group" delegates cannot see the Agent Dashboard. Delegator leaves org All delegates immediately lose access. Recovery: another leader must re-delegate to those users. Plan for Delegator Departures If a key delegator (e.g., a VP or CIO) leaves the organisation, all 75 delegates could lose dashboard access simultaneously. Document who has delegated to whom and ensure at least two senior leaders have overlapping delegation lists for continuity.

heading: 3. Copilot Analytics Reports
content: Copilot Analytics Reports are pre-built reports available at insights.cloud.microsoft → Reports . They provide deeper analysis than the dashboard pages without requiring Power BI setup.

heading: Report Types
content: Type Examples Description Out-of-the-box Copilot Studio Agents, Copilot for Sales Adoption Pre-built by Microsoft. Available automatically once prerequisites are met. Shared Custom reports published by your analysts Analysts with partition access can create queries and publish results as shared reports visible to others in the Reports section.

heading: Access
content: Anyone with Dashboard access (direct or delegated) can view reports. Analysts with a global partition can create and share reports. Global Admins have full access. No additional setup is required beyond what is already configured for the Copilot Dashboard.

heading: 4. Advanced Power BI Templates
content: When the dashboard and built-in reports are not enough, Microsoft provides Power BI templates that connect directly to Viva Insights query data. Use the decision guide below to pick the right template.

heading: 5. Agent Dashboard (Preview)
content: The Agent Dashboard preview provides visibility into supported Copilot Studio and declarative-agent activity for eligible users with "Your company" scope. Managers and "Your group" delegates do not receive Agent Dashboard access. Preview prerequisite conflict and cloud limits Connect to the Agent Dashboard (preview) (Last updated 16 June 2026) and Copilot Analytics introduction (Last updated 9 February 2026) require at least 50 Microsoft 365 Copilot licences plus agent activity. The broader Dashboard article (Last updated 1 July 2026) also lists a 50-Viva-Insights route. Use 50 Copilot licences plus activity as the conservative prerequisite; validate a Viva-Insights-only tenant through its UI, Message Center, and Microsoft Support. The Agent Dashboard is unavailable in GCC and other national or regional clouds. Preview behavior, metrics, filters, and data handling can change. Confirm current support in Supported Microsoft 365 environments for Viva Insights features (Last updated 30 June 2026) before using this procedure.

heading: Key Metrics
content: Metric Definition Active agents Number of agents that received at least one user interaction in the period Active users Unique users who interacted with any agent Agent responses Total number of responses generated by agents Returning users Users who interacted with agents in both the current and previous period % of Copilot users using agents Agent active users ÷ total Copilot active users — shows agent penetration Copilot Credits Credits consumed by agent interactions (relevant for pay-as-you-go billing)

heading: Unique Insights
content: Insight What It Tells You Most popular agent Agent with the highest number of active users Most versatile agent Agent used across the widest variety of use cases or groups Most shared agent Agent shared with the most users or groups in the tenant

heading: Filters
content: Filter Options Agent creator type User, Your org, Microsoft, Partner Agent source Filter by where the agent was built or published

heading: Scenario 1: Post-Launch Adoption Review
content: Your organisation deployed three custom agents last month. Use the Agent Dashboard to answer: How many users actually tried each agent? (Active users per agent) Are people coming back? (Returning users) Which agent is getting the most traction? (Most popular agent) Action: Double down on training for the popular agent; investigate why low-adoption agents are being ignored (discoverability? relevance?).

heading: Scenario 2: Cost Governance for Pay-As-You-Go
content: Your tenant uses PAYG billing for agent interactions. Use the Agent Dashboard to answer: How many Copilot Credits are being consumed? (Credits metric) Which agents are the biggest consumers? (Sort by responses) Are partner-built agents driving unexpected costs? (Filter by creator type: Partner) Action: Set credit alerts, review high-consumption agents with their owners, consider blocking non-essential agents.

heading: Scenario 3: Shadow AI Detection
content: Users are creating their own agents without IT approval. Use the Agent Dashboard to answer: How many user-created agents exist? (Filter by creator type: User) Are any user-created agents being widely shared? (Most shared agent) Do any of these agents have external data connections that violate policy? Action: Follow the Detect → Act workflow below.

heading: Detect → Act Workflow
content: Stage Tool Action Detect Agent Dashboard Identify agents that are unapproved, high-cost, or widely shared without governance review Decide Governance process Evaluate the agent against your organisation's AI governance policy (data access, compliance, purpose) Act Quick M365 Admin Centre Immediate actions: Block (disable agent), Approve (whitelist), or Review (flag for governance team) Act Policy Power Platform Admin Centre Long-term controls: apply DLP policies to restrict connector access, enforce environment boundaries, set maker policies

heading: 6. Suggested Review Cadence
content: Operational validation before diagnosis Before treating a missing page, changed metric, or sudden data dip as tenant misconfiguration, check Microsoft 365 admin center Service Health for incidents and Message Center for rollout, preview, or reporting changes. Record the notice ID and date alongside the report, then retest the affected surface after the published recovery or rollout window. heading: 1. Prerequisites
content: Before you can create Copilot analytics queries and connect Power BI templates, the following must be in place: Prerequisite Details Viva Insights licence Minimum 50 licences in the tenant for Advanced Analytics. heading: Data Requirements
content: Timing matters. Plan your data collection window before Copilot rolls out if you need before/after analysis: Data Requirement Minimum Notes Org data upload Required Enables segmentation by department, agency, location, job function, and other attributes. Pre-adoption baseline 4 weeks Needed for before/after comparison in the Copilot Impact template. Must be collected before Copilot licence assignment. Post-adoption data 4 weeks Minimum period after Copilot rollout for meaningful usage patterns to emerge. Plan Ahead for Before/After Analysis If Copilot has already been rolled out across your tenant, before/after comparison may not be possible . The Impact template requires pre-adoption collaboration data as a baseline. Plan your analytics strategy before licence assignment to ensure you capture baseline data.

heading: 2. Available Copilot PBI Templates
content: Microsoft provides several Power BI templates purpose-built for Copilot analytics. Each template creates a pre-built query with predefined metrics and a corresponding .pbit file for Power BI Desktop. Template Purpose Key Metrics M365 Copilot Impact Primary ROI and productivity analysis Assisted hours, assisted value, before/after behavioural comparison, Copilot vs non-Copilot user comparison M365 Copilot Adoption Usage trends and feature adoption depth Active users over time, per-app usage, feature-level adoption, usage intensity trends Copilot Headlines (preview) Auto-scanned executive insights Power users, adoption benchmarks, auto-generated insight summaries Copilot Business Impact Correlate Copilot usage with business outcomes Business KPI correlation (requires business outcome data upload via org data) Copilot Studio Agents Agent usage and impact analysis Agent session outcomes, credits consumed, user engagement with custom agents Copilot Employee Experience (Glint) Correlate Copilot usage with employee sentiment Usage patterns mapped to Glint survey responses (requires Viva Glint licence and integration) Where to Find These Templates The official Copilot templates are not ...

heading: 3. Step-by-Step: Create a Report
content: This walkthrough uses the M365 Copilot Impact template as the example, but the process is similar for all templates.

heading: Step 1 — Sign In to the Analyst Experience
content: Navigate to analysis.insights.viva.office.com and sign in with your organisational account that has the Insights Analyst role.

heading: Step 2 — Start a New Analysis
content: Select Create analysis → Templates → Copilot → choose the desired template (e.g., M365 Copilot Impact ) → Set up analysis .

heading: Step 3 — Configure Query Settings
content: Set the following: Query name — use a descriptive name (e.g., "Copilot Impact Q1 2026 — All Agencies") Time period — select the analysis window (up to 13 months of historical data) Auto-refresh — enable if you want the query to re-run automatically on a schedule Description — optional but recommended for team documentation

heading: Step 4 — Review Predefined Metrics
content: The template pre-selects the required metrics (shown as grey tags — these cannot be removed). You can optionally add extra metrics to include in the query output.

heading: Step 5 — Set Employee Filters
content: Apply filters to scope the query population: "Is Active" — always recommended to exclude departed employees Org attributes — filter by department, agency, location, or any uploaded org data attribute as needed

heading: Step 6 — Add Organisational Attributes
content: Select up to 20 organisational attributes to include in the query output. These attributes become available as group-by and filter dimensions in Power BI. Include any attribute you may want to slice by later — you cannot add them after the query runs.

heading: Step 7 — Run the Query
content: Select Run . Depending on population size and time window, the query may take minutes to hours to complete. You will receive a notification when results are ready. Data Quality Warnings During query setup, the system may flag data quality issues: Yellow warning — low quality or partial coverage for an attribute. The query will still run. Red warning — missing attribute entirely. The query will run but that attribute will not be usable for segmentation. Address data quality issues by working with your Insights Admin to update the org data upload.

heading: 4. Connect Power BI
content: Once your query completes, connect it to the Power BI template:

heading: Step 1 — Download the Template
content: From the query results page in Viva Insights, download the .pbit template file.

heading: Step 2 — Open in Power BI Desktop
content: Open the downloaded .pbit file in Power BI Desktop. When prompted, paste: Partition ID — found in the Viva Insights analyst experience Query ID — found on the query results page

heading: Step 3 — Set Minimum Group Size
content: Set the minimum group size for privacy thresholds (e.g., 10). Groups smaller than this threshold will be suppressed in the report. Click Load .

heading: Step 4 — Sign In
content: When prompted for credentials, select Organisational account and sign in with the same account used to run the query.

heading: Step 5 — Configure Report Settings
content: After data loads, configure the report settings on the Settings page: Time period — define the reporting window within the query's date range Group-by attribute — select the primary organisational attribute for segmentation (e.g., Department, Agency) Active user definition — set the threshold for what constitutes an "active" Copilot user Language — set report language

heading: Explore the Report Pages
content: The Copilot Impact template includes several pre-built pages: Summary — high-level overview of Copilot impact across the organisation Before vs After — compares collaboration patterns before and after Copilot adoption Copilot vs Non-Copilot — compares metrics between users with and without Copilot licences Two-Group Comparison — compare two organisational segments side by side Research Insights — deeper statistical analysis and trend exploration

heading: 5. Programmatic Reporting Options
content: Choose the reporting path based on the question, population, privacy model, and automation requirement. These options are not interchangeable: a dashboard export is not an Advanced query, Microsoft Graph does not cover unlicensed Copilot Chat usage, and audit events are not equivalent to adoption metrics. Option Best for Population and scope Key limits Copilot Dashboard export (preview) Offline adoption analysis, custom communications, and short- or medium-term trend reporting De-identified user-level rows for licensed Microsoft 365 Copilot and unlicensed Copilot Chat usage; available only to users with global Dashboard access At least 50 Copilot or Viva Insights licences assigned; fixed, non-customisable dataset; no Copilot assisted hours; secure handling required Advanced Reporting query Custom metrics, filters, org attributes, cohort analysis, and Power BI templates Measured employees and rows available to the Insights Analyst's assigned partition Insights Analyst role and partition access required; query design, privacy thresholds, data quality, and refresh ownership remain operational responsibilities Microsoft Graph Copilot report API (v1.0) Scheduled licensed-user usage r...

heading: Dashboard export: fixed user-level dataset
content: The Copilot Dashboard's Export data feature is public preview. Week-level export provides the last six months , refreshes weekly, and can be up to six days old. Day-level export provides the last 28 days , refreshes daily, and can be up to three days old. Both remove personal identifiers and replace them with anonymized IDs; neither can be customized. The feature currently excludes Copilot assisted hours. Global access carries a wider data-handling responsibility Eligible exporters include senior leaders with global access, users manually assigned global access, delegates of globally scoped users, Insights Analysts with global access, and Microsoft 365 Global Administrators. Managers and their delegates with team-level views cannot export. Treat every download as a governed user-level dataset even though direct identifiers are removed.

heading: Advanced queries: customizable and partition-scoped
content: Use Advanced Reporting when you need selected metrics, employee filters, organizational attributes, a defined privacy threshold, or a reusable Power BI model. Microsoft documents more than 100 Copilot metrics. Unlike the fixed Dashboard export, the available query population is constrained by the analyst's assigned partition. For Copilot metrics, design within the documented 13-month history and account for data freshness.

heading: Microsoft Graph v1.0: licensed users only
content: The current Copilot report root exposes both a user-count summary and user detail in v1.0 : GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/copilot/reports/getMicrosoft365CopilotUserCountSummary(period='D30') GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/copilot/reports/getMicrosoft365CopilotUsageUserDetail(period='D30')?$format=application/json API property Current requirement Least-privileged delegated or application permission Reports.Read.All Report shapes getMicrosoft365CopilotUserCountSummary for aggregate user counts; getMicrosoft365CopilotUsageUserDetail for licensed-user activity detail Supported period values D7 , D30 , D90 , D180 , ALL ALL meaning Returns report periods for 7, 30, 90, and 180 days; it is not an unlimited-retention promise Population Users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot licence only Prompt detail Per-user Copilot prompt counts across tenants are not supported National clouds Available in Global service; not available in US Government L4, US Government L5 (DoD), or China operated by 21Vianet Production version Use v1.0 ; Microsoft states that /beta APIs are subject to change and unsupported for production

heading: Unlicensed Copilot Chat: admin report or audit
content: Unlicensed Copilot Chat usage is not returned by the Graph Copilot reports API. Use the Microsoft 365 admin center Copilot Chat Usage report for the current operational report. For programmatic event extraction, Microsoft documents Microsoft Purview Audit , Search-UnifiedAuditLog in PowerShell, and the Office 365 Management Activity API . Audit is an alternative source, not a metric-compatible substitute Audit data supports compliance and event analysis. Do not present event counts as if they were the admin report's active-user or adoption measures. Define event types, deduplication, retention, permissions, latency, identity handling, and aggregation before publishing a trend.

heading: Architecture decision
content: Use the Dashboard export for a governed, fixed adoption dataset that includes current unlicensed Chat and usage-intensity measures. Use Advanced queries when partition scope, custom metrics, filters, organizational attributes, or Power BI templates are required. Use Graph v1.0 for scheduled licensed-user usage integrations with Reports.Read.All . Use the admin report for current unlicensed Chat reporting; use Purview/Management Activity only when event-level programmatic extraction is the actual requirement. Persist only the minimum required data, apply access and retention controls, record source/period/refresh time, and validate totals against the source experience.

heading: 6.2 Agent Assisted Hours and Value
content: Copilot assisted hours and Agent Assisted Hours are modeled estimates. They use different activity inputs and assumptions; neither is a timesheet, realized saving, budget reduction, or proof of business value. Measure Model and assumptions How to use it Microsoft 365 Copilot assisted hours/value Actions are multiplied by research-derived time-savings factors. Value multiplies estimated hours by an average hourly rate, set to US$72 by default and adjustable in the calculator. Microsoft states the methodology will evolve. Scenario estimate for adoption planning; disclose the rate, multipliers, period, diagnostics coverage, and sensitivity range. Maker-led Agent Assisted Impact Inputs are supplied from Copilot Studio and displayed in Viva Insights; maker-led values are not editable in Viva Insights. Validate the maker's baseline, calculation, unit, and evidence outside the report before using the figure. Inferred conversational-agent AAH (knowledge-source references + weighted sessions without knowledge sources) × time-savings multiplier . Resolved sessions receive full weight; escalated and abandoned sessions are weighted at 0.7. The default information-retrieval multiplier is six...

heading: 7. Publish & Share Reports
content: Once your report is built (Sections 1–4), you have two delivery surfaces for getting it in front of leaders. They are not interchangeable — they differ in where recipients view the report, what access it grants them, and how well they respect agency boundaries. Pick the surface that matches your distribution model, then follow the matching sub-section below.   7.1 Publish via Viva Insights 7.2 Share via Power BI Where recipients view Viva Insights web app → Reports tab Power BI service ( app.powerbi.com ) or a Power BI app Best for Fast, native distribution to leaders who already have Copilot Dashboard access Controlled, named-audience distribution — especially multi-agency Viewer licence Viva Insights service plan (included with Copilot) Power BI Pro / PPU, or Premium capacity Respects agency isolation Risk — see the 5.1 warning Yes — per-workspace, optionally RLS Implementing the Isolation model? Use 7.2, not 7.1 If you are delivering reports under the Multi-Agency Isolation Architecture , distribute to agency heads via Power BI (Section 7.2) . Do not use Viva Insights Publish (7.1) to reach agency leaders: enabling a manager so they can receive a published report grants ...

heading: 7.1 Publish via Viva Insights
content: You can share query results with business leaders directly from Viva Insights using the Publish feature. This is the fastest route for leaders who already have Copilot Dashboard access.

heading: Step 1 — Select Publish
content: In the Viva Insights analyst experience, navigate to your completed query and select Publish .

heading: Step 2 — Set Report Metadata
content: Enter: Title — the report title that recipients will see Publisher name — your name or team name Description — brief summary of what the report covers

heading: Step 3 — Choose Scope
content: Select one of: Unfiltered original — publishes the full dataset. All recipients see the same data. Filter by manager hierarchy — each recipient sees only data for their reporting line.

heading: Step 4 — Set Expiry Date
content: Set when the published report expires. Maximum is 365 days from publication.

heading: Step 5 — Publish
content: Confirm and publish. Recipients receive an email notification with a link to view the report. No Auto-Notification Before Expiry Published reports expire silently — there is no automatic notification sent before a report expires. Set a calendar reminder to re-publish before the expiry date to avoid gaps in leader access. Multi-Agency Tenants: Manager Hierarchy Risk Enabling a manager so they can receive a published report also grants them Copilot Dashboard access — including the "Your company" scope, which shows aggregated data across all agencies , not just their own reporting line. In a multi-agency tenant this breaks agency isolation. For agency-head delivery, use Power BI sharing (Section 7.2) instead, and reserve Viva Insights Publish for Central IT who already have tenant-wide dashboard access. See the Multi-Agency Setup Guide and the Isolation playbook (Phase 4b).

heading: 7.2 Share via Power BI
content: Unlike Publish via Viva Insights (Section 7.1) , which distributes results natively inside Viva Insights, this method uses Power BI's own sharing . It is the preferred method for multi-agency tenants because it delivers a report to named people without granting them any Copilot Dashboard access — whereas 7.1's "Filter by manager hierarchy" option can (see its warning). New to Power BI? Start Here This section assumes an Insights Analyst has already built the report in Sections 1–4 (run a query, connect the template, save the file). If you are an admin handling only distribution , you can start at Step 1 below with a report file the analyst hands you. Three Power BI terms used in this section: .pbix — the finished report file. When the analyst loads data into the .pbit template (Section 4) and saves it, it becomes a .pbix . That is the file you publish. Workspace — a shared container in the Power BI service that holds reports and controls who can see them. Dataset — the data behind a report; publishing a .pbix uploads the report and its dataset together.

heading: Step 1 — Publish the .pbix to the Power BI Service
content: In Power BI Desktop, select Publish and choose a destination workspace (not "My workspace", which can't be shared with others). The report and its dataset upload to app.powerbi.com .

heading: Step 2 — Use a Dedicated Workspace per Audience
content: Create one workspace per agency or audience (e.g., Agency A — Copilot Analytics ). Keeping each agency's reports in a separate workspace is the cleanest way to control who sees what. Store all .pbix files here rather than on individual machines so reporting survives staff changes.

heading: Step 3 — Assign Stakeholders via Workspace Roles
content: In app.powerbi.com , open the workspace → select Manage access → Add people or groups , then assign a workspace role from the table below. Prefer adding an Entra security group over individuals, so access is managed centrally and consistently with your other governance controls. Role Can do Assign to Viewer View and interact with reports only Most stakeholders (agency leaders) Contributor Create/edit content; can't manage access Co-analysts who build reports Member Edit content and add others (below Admin) Trusted leads who help run the workspace Admin Full control, including deleting the workspace The owning analyst / Central IT Cleaner Distribution for Wide Audiences For a large, read-only audience, publish the workspace as a Power BI app — a packaged, read-only view of selected reports (not a desktop or mobile application). Give the audience access to the app rather than the workspace, so recipients get a curated view without seeing the underlying workspace contents.

heading: Step 4 — Confirm Viewer Licensing
content: Power BI sharing is licensed separately from Viva Insights and Copilot: Each viewer needs a Power BI Pro or Premium Per User (PPU) licence; or if the workspace sits on a Power BI Premium capacity (P or F SKU) , viewers with a free Power BI licence can view the reports — removing per-viewer costs. Confirm with your licensing owner. Multi-Agency: Keep Boundaries Intact Grant each agency workspace's Viewer role only to that agency's leaders — via an agency-scoped Entra group. If multiple agencies must share one workspace, add Row-Level Security (RLS) as a secondary control. The full agency-isolation delivery procedure (including the Publish Reports pitfall) is in the Isolation Architecture playbook , Phase 4b.

heading: 8. Ongoing Maintenance
content: Keep your Copilot analytics current with these recurring tasks: Refresh org data monthly — work with your Insights Admin to ensure organisational data (new hires, transfers, departures) stays up to date. Stale org data leads to inaccurate segmentation. Re-run queries periodically — either enable auto-refresh during query setup or manually re-run queries on a monthly or quarterly cadence to capture current trends. Update PBI reports — when Microsoft releases updated template versions, download the new .pbit and reconnect to benefit from new metrics and visualisations. Share via Publish Reports or Power BI workspaces — for ongoing distribution, either re-publish through Viva Insights or save .pbix files to a shared Power BI workspace (see Section 7.2 for the step-by-step).

heading: 9. Analyst Departure — Transfer Guide
content: When an Insights Analyst leaves the organisation or changes roles, follow this checklist to ensure continuity: Item What Happens Action Required Completed query results Persist in Viva Insights — a new analyst with partition access can view them No action required (results are not tied to the analyst's account) Scheduled / recurring queries May stop running when the account is disabled or deleted Recreate scheduled queries under the new analyst's account Published reports Remain accessible until their expiry date, but cannot be renewed by a different analyst Re-publish reports under the new analyst's account before the originals expire Power BI files (.pbix) Stored locally on the analyst's machine — lost if not transferred Transfer all .pbix files to a shared Power BI workspace before account deletion Partition access Role assignment is per-user — does not transfer automatically Insights Admin must assign partition access to the replacement analyst Redundancy Is Essential Always have at least two analysts with partition access. Store all .pbix files in a shared Power BI workspace rather than on individual machines. This prevents data loss and reporting gaps during transitions.

heading: 1. Purpose
content: This playbook is an operational guide for managing Microsoft Copilot licence lifecycle events and billing models. It covers what happens to analytics data when licences are assigned, removed, or reassigned — and provides step-by-step billing configuration procedures. Use this document alongside the main Copilot Analytics guide to ensure licence changes do not create gaps in your analytics reporting or unexpected billing charges.

heading: 4. Copilot Credits — Understanding Consumption
content: Copilot Credits are the consumption currency for Copilot Studio agent interactions and certain M365 Copilot Chat scenarios. Understanding what costs credits — and what doesn't — is essential for budget management.

heading: What Costs Credits
content: Agent responses using Copilot Studio capabilities beyond zero-rated usage Tenant data grounding (SharePoint, Graph) in M365 Copilot Chat (web-based) Custom agent actions, plugins, and connector invocations Generative answers using non-M365 knowledge sources

heading: Zero-Rated (No Credits Required)
content: Classic (deterministic) answers in Copilot Studio agents Generative answers using Microsoft Graph tenant grounding when used by M365 Copilot licensed users in M365 apps (Word, Excel, Teams, etc.) Standard Copilot-in-app interactions for licensed M365 Copilot users

heading: Credit Mechanics
content: Credits reset monthly — there is no rollover of unused credits to the next month. Overage behaviour: when credits are exhausted, model runs are blocked until more credits are purchased or allocated. Credits are pooled at the tenant level for prepaid plans, or billed per-environment for pay-as-you-go .

heading: Consumption Dashboard in Insights
content: The Consumption Dashboard is one of Microsoft's six Copilot Analytics areas. It helps eligible global or group-scoped viewers understand Copilot Credit consumption by supported service and organizational scope. Use it for operational trend and scope analysis, not as the invoice, entitlement ledger, or official billing system of record. Reconcile its output with Power Platform Admin Center, Microsoft 365 admin center credit reports, and Azure billing records before chargeback or accrual decisions. Access depends on the documented role and scope model; delegation is not a substitute for Consumption Dashboard eligibility. Source: How to use the Consumption Dashboard in Insights (Last updated 1 July 2026) and Copilot Analytics introduction (Last updated 9 February 2026). Warning: Credits do not roll over. Monitor consumption weekly to avoid unexpected service interruptions at month-end.

heading: Emergency: Credits Exhausted — Agents Blocked
content: If agents stop responding and users see errors, follow this resolution path: Confirm the cause: Navigate to Power Platform Admin Center → Billing → Licenses. Check if the credit balance shows 0 or "Exhausted". Immediate fix — Add credits: Pay-as-you-go: Verify the Azure subscription linked to the billing policy is active and has available budget. If the subscription spending limit was hit, increase it in Azure Portal → Cost Management . Prepaid: Purchase additional Copilot Credits via your Microsoft account team or the M365 Admin Center → Billing → Purchase Services. Credits are applied within 1–2 hours. Prevent recurrence — Set up alerts: In PPAC : Billing → select the billing policy → configure usage notifications at 50%, 75%, and 90% thresholds. In Azure (for pay-as-you-go): Cost Management → Budgets → create a budget with action group email alerts. Review consumption: Use the Copilot Credits Activity Report in Admin Center to identify which agents consumed the most credits and whether any can be optimised. Reference: Copilot Studio Message and Credit Management

heading: 5. PPAC Billing Configuration
content: Follow these steps to configure and monitor billing in the Power Platform Admin Center: Navigate to admin.powerplatform.microsoft.com and sign in as a Power Platform Administrator. Go to Licensing → Copilot Studio in the left navigation pane. Review the Summary view: Prepaid capacity — total credits purchased, credits consumed, credits remaining Pay-as-you-go — active billing plans linked to Azure subscriptions Consumption trend — daily/weekly/monthly usage graphs View Capacity consumption by product to see breakdown across Copilot Studio, M365 Copilot Chat, and other eligible products. To allocate prepaid credits to specific environments: Select Allocate capacity Choose the target environment Enter the number of credits to allocate Confirm the allocation To create a pay-as-you-go billing policy: Select New billing policy Link to an Azure subscription and resource group Assign the billing policy to one or more environments Set spending limits if desired Use the Agent Usage Estimator tool to forecast credit consumption for planned agents: microsoft.github.io/copilot-studio-estimator Reference: Manage Copilot Studio Messages Capacity

heading: 6. Admin Center Billing Reports
content: The Microsoft 365 Admin Center provides a Copilot Credits report that gives detailed visibility into credit consumption across your organisation.

heading: Accessing the Report
content: Sign in to admin.microsoft.com . Navigate to Reports → Usage → Copilot Credits . Review the report components: Total credits used: Aggregate consumption for the billing period Time series: Cumulative and daily credit consumption charts Per-user breakdown: Credits consumed by individual users Per-agent breakdown: Credits consumed by each published agent Per-billing-policy: Consumption grouped by billing policy Tip: Export the per-user breakdown to CSV and join it with your org data file to produce agency-level or cost-centre-level chargeback reports. Reference: Copilot Credits Activity Report

heading: 7. Multi-Agency Chargeback Model
content: For organisations with multiple agencies, departments, or cost centres, a chargeback model distributes Copilot costs to the consuming business units.

heading: Option A: Org Data–Based Attribution
content: Upload org data with an Agency (or CostCentre ) attribute via Viva Insights or PPAC. Export the Copilot Credits report filtered by user from the M365 Admin Center. Join user-level consumption data with org data to map each user's credit usage to their agency. Aggregate by agency to produce the chargeback summary.

heading: Option B: Separate Billing Policies
content: Create separate billing policies in PPAC — one per agency. Link each billing policy to a different Azure subscription or cost centre. Assign environments to the appropriate billing policy based on the owning agency. Each agency's Azure subscription is billed directly for their consumption.

heading: Quarterly Reconciliation Process
content: At the end of each quarter, export consumption data from both PPAC and M365 Admin Center. Reconcile prepaid credit consumption against allocations per agency. Reconcile pay-as-you-go charges against Azure invoices. Review and adjust allocations based on actual usage trends. Produce a chargeback summary report for finance teams. Note: Option B (separate billing policies) provides cleaner cost separation but requires more administrative overhead. Option A is simpler to maintain for organisations with fewer than 10 agencies. Agent Billing Governance: For detailed credit mechanics, safe scaling patterns, irreversible billing decisions, and the billing boundary design framework, see the Copilot & Agent Governance project — specifically the Agent Billing & Credit Governance guide.

heading: Monthly Tasks
content: Review licence utilisation in the M365 Admin Center — identify unused licences Review credit consumption in PPAC — check against budget thresholds Reallocate unused licences to requesting users or business units Check for blocked agent runs due to credit exhaustion Review new licence assignments and confirm dashboard data appearance Check Microsoft 365 admin center Service Health and Message Center before attributing report gaps, metric changes, or missing preview features to configuration

heading: Quarterly Tasks
content: Audit exclusion list s in Viva Insights — remove departed employees, add new exclusions Review billing policies — ensure environments are assigned to correct policies Reconcile chargeback — produce agency-level cost reports (see Multi-Agency Isolation Architecture for partition-based chargeback setup) Assess scale needs — forecast licence and credit requirements for next quarter Review lifecycle events — confirm org data CSV reflects current organisational structure Document any licence model changes (e.g., shift from prepaid to pay-as-you-go) Best Practice: Create calendar reminders for these tasks and assign a named owner to each checklist item. Governance lapses most often occur when tasks lack clear ownership.

heading: Advanced Viva Insights — Collaboration Analysis Guide
content: Using the Advanced Analysis Workbench for Workplace Collaboration Patterns Beyond Copilot

heading: 1. Purpose
content: This guide covers using Viva Insights Advanced Analysis to understand workplace collaboration patterns beyond Copilot . The same analyst workbench, governed organizational data , the same privacy settings, and the same Power BI workflow — but with different metrics and templates focused on meetings, focus time, wellbeing, and manager effectiveness. Collaboration analysis provides the organisational context that makes Copilot impact data meaningful. Without understanding baseline collaboration patterns, it is impossible to measure whether Copilot is genuinely improving how people work.

heading: 2. Relationship to Copilot Analytics
content: Collaboration analysis and Copilot analytics share the same infrastructure. Understanding their relationship helps analysts maximise both capabilities. Both query types can be active concurrently. Key Insight: Existing Viva Insights organizational data, partitions, and privacy settings can be reused, but first verify that the tenant's current data source is healthy, the analyst is assigned to the intended partition, and the requested report is supported in ...

How much time is spent in recurring vs. ad-hoc meetings? Are schedules flexible? Do employees take breaks and connect with colleagues? Supported Impact Explorer Impact estimation of Viva Insights actions Did meeting reduction initiatives save time? heading: 4. Key Behavioural Metrics
content: The following table groups the core behavioural metrics available for collaboration analysis. These metrics can be used in templates, custom queries, and filters. Category Metrics Meeting Hours Meeting hours, recurring meeting hours, conflicting meeting hours, multitasking meeting hours, long meeting hours, large meeting hours Focus Time Focus hours, uninterrupted focus hours, booked focus hours (via Viva Insights focus plan) After-Hours After-hours collaboration hours, after-hours meeting hours, after-hours email hours, after-hours chat hours Email Email hours, emails sent, emails read Chat & Calls Chat hours, chats sent, call hours, calls made Manager Interaction Manager 1:1 meeting hours, manager co-attendance hours Collaboration Scope Internal collaboration hours, external collaboration hours, cross-group collaboration hours, network size Metric Tip: "Multitasking meeting hours" captures time in meetings where the employee was also actively sending emails or chats — a strong indicator of meeting overload or low meeting relevance. Reference: Viva Insights Metric Definitions

heading: 5. Custom Query Capabilities
content: Beyond the pre-built templates, analysts can create fully custom queries with tailored metrics, filters, and groupings.

heading: Custom Metric Features
content: Filter metrics by day of week (e.g., meeting hours on Fridays only) Filter by meeting duration (e.g., meetings longer than 60 minutes) Filter by attendee count (e.g., meetings with more than 10 attendees) Filter by organisational attributes from org data (e.g., department, location, level) Clone existing metrics and modify filters for tailored analysis

heading: Copilot Assistant
content: The Viva Insights analyst workbench includes a Copilot assistant that can help you choose appropriate metrics and filters based on your analysis goals. Describe what you want to measure, and the assistant will suggest relevant metrics and configurations. Power User Tip: Clone a standard metric (e.g., "Meeting hours"), then add a filter for "recurring = true" and "duration > 60 minutes" to create a metric that specifically captures time in long recurring meetings — a common target for optimisation. Reference: Custom Metrics in Viva Insights

heading: 7. How to Create a Collaboration Report
content: The workflow for creating a collaboration report follows the same pattern as Copilot Analytics templates. If you have created a Copilot query before, this process will be familiar. Sign in to analysis.insights.viva.office.com with your Insights Analyst account. Select Create analysis from the left navigation, then choose Templates . Select a collaboration template — for example, Ways of Working . Configure the query: Name: Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "Q1 2026 Ways of Working – All Agencies") Time period: Select the analysis window — collaboration data supports up to 27 months Auto-refresh: Enable if you want the query to re-run automatically on a schedule Review the predefined metrics included in the template. Optionally add custom metrics using the Add metric button. Set employee filters to scope the analysis (e.g., specific departments, locations, or levels). Select which org attributes to include in the output for grouping. Select Run to execute the query. Processing time depends on population size and time period — typically 30 minutes to several hours. Once complete, download the .pbit file (Power BI template) from the query results page. Open the .pbit file in Power ...

heading: Scenario 1: Meeting Culture Audit
content: Template: Meeting Effectiveness Goal: Identify meeting overload, long recurring meetings, and multitasking patterns across the organisation. Approach: Run the Meeting Effectiveness template with a 6-month time window. Analyse metrics: recurring meeting hours, meetings > 60 minutes, multitasking hours. Group by department or business unit to identify hotspots. Calculate potential time savings: if average employee spends 4 hours/week in meetings that could be emails, that's X hours reclaimed per person per week. Outcome: Data-driven recommendations to reclaim meeting time, backed by actual behavioural data rather than surveys.

heading: Scenario 2: Wellbeing Baseline Before Copilot Rollout
content: Template: Wellbeing – Balance & Flexibility Goal: Capture focus time, after-hours work, and work-life balance metrics before Copilot deployment. Then compare after 3 months using the Copilot Impact template. Approach: Run the Wellbeing template covering the 4 weeks immediately before Copilot licence assignment. Record the baseline period and cohort before comparing post-deployment results; see the Analyst Playbook: Business Impact setup and interpretation . Record baseline metrics: average focus hours, after-hours collaboration hours, weekend work hours. After 3 months of Copilot usage, run the Copilot Impact template for the same population. Compare pre- and post-deployment metrics to quantify Copilot's impact on employee wellbeing. Outcome: Quantifiable evidence of whether Copilot improved, maintained, or negatively affected employee wellbeing metrics.

heading: Scenario 3: Manager Effectiveness Programme
content: Template: Manager Effectiveness Goal: Identify managers with low 1:1 time or high span of control. Feed insights into leadership development initiatives. Approach: Run the Manager Effectiveness template for the past 6 months. Analyse metrics: manager 1:1 meeting hours, team size (span of control), team after-hours patterns. Filter to managers with fewer than 30 minutes of 1:1 time per direct report per month. Cross-reference with team wellbeing metrics to identify teams with both low manager engagement and high after-hours work. Outcome: Targeted list of managers who would benefit from coaching programmes, with behavioural evidence to support the recommendation.

heading: 9. Data Retention
content: Understanding data retention windows is important for planning analysis timeframes and comparing historical trends. Data Type Maximum Retention Notes Collaboration metrics (meetings, email, chat, focus time) Up to 27 months Initially 13 months, then grows through weekly refreshes to a maximum of 27 months. No collaboration data older than 27 months is retained. Copilot metrics (adoption, usage, impact, sentiment) Up to 13 months Viva Insights retains Microsoft 365 Copilot metrics for no more than 13 months. Practical Implication: Collaboration analysis can look further back in history than Copilot analysis. This means you can establish a rich historical baseline of collaboration patterns — even if Copilot was deployed recently. Use the longer retention window to identify seasonal trends, reorganisation impacts, and long-term cultural shifts. Source: Advanced analysis privacy — Data retention for active tenants (Microsoft Learn, updated 10 December 2025).

heading: 10. Operational Validation
content: Cadence Check Pass / escalation rule Before each new analysis Confirm tenant cloud, template availability, analyst role/partition, current org-data source, privacy threshold, and approved purpose. Proceed only when each dependency is evidenced. Stop if Message Center and UI disagree. After an org-data or partition change Rerun a known query; compare population, key attributes, suppression, and partition scope with the pre-change baseline. Pass when counts and scope reconcile. Roll back/correct the data or partition and preserve logs if they do not. Monthly Review Message Center and Microsoft 365 Service Health for Viva Insights changes/incidents; check query failures, data freshness, and auto-refresh status. Record notice/incident IDs and dates. Do not diagnose a tenant defect until current service and rollout notices are excluded. Quarterly Review analyst assignments, partitions, query audit logs, exported report locations, retention, and the supported-environments matrix. Remove unnecessary access/copies. Escalate undocumented product behavior with query ID, partition ID, UTC time, cloud, expected/actual counts, and screenshots. Sources: Advanced analysis privacy — Advanced an...

heading: Step 1 — Open Insights and confirm scope
content: Open the Viva Insights web app and select the Consumption Dashboard . Confirm the scope before interpreting any value: Company is available only to global-partition Insights Analysts and Microsoft 365 Global Administrators. Group limits the view to the manager's group. Organization and Job function use the same employee-attribute sources as the Copilot Dashboard. Time period is currently month to date. Organization can be derived from Microsoft Entra ID or uploaded organizational data. Job function depends on the corresponding uploaded organizational attribute; a missing field can produce an empty or incomplete filter.

heading: Step 2 — Read the summary cards
content: Metric Operational meaning Validation question Active users Unique users who took at least one action in Copilot Cowork or the Work IQ API Did covered activity occur in this scope during the current month? Total Copilot credit usage Credits consumed by covered activity in the selected scope Does the direction align with the official billing view, allowing for scope and timing? Total session count Copilot Cowork or Work IQ API sessions initiated by users Did a rollout, campaign, integration, or automation change session volume?

heading: Step 3 — Analyze trends and service drivers
content: Use the trend controls to change the plotted metric, break it down by group, organization, or job function, and choose the available date granularity. Then use the service breakdown to identify which covered service and employee segment account for the largest share. Treat this as a prioritization signal, then validate against service ownership and business context.

heading: Step 4 — Review usage intensity
content: Usage intensity shows whether consumption is broadly distributed or concentrated. Review these segments: All users Top 1 percent of users Top 5 percent of users Top 6–25 percent of users Top 26–50 percent of users For each segment, compare total credits, average credits per user, average credits per session, average sessions per user, and active users. A concentrated distribution is a prompt to investigate workload design, automation, value, and policy fit; it is not by itself evidence of misuse.

heading: Step 5 — Review spending-policy limits
content: Use the spending-policy table to identify the top policies in use, average credits used per user, the displayed spending limit, and the number of users near or at that limit. Contact the policy owner before users are depleted. The dashboard reports the condition; policy configuration and capacity enforcement occur in the relevant administration and billing surfaces. Capacity has contractual and service consequences Copilot Studio documentation states that purchased capacity is enforced monthly, unused Copilot Credits do not carry over, and exceeding purchased capacity can lead to technical enforcement and service denial. Confirm the applicable licensing model, included usage, zero-rated scenarios, and current terms in the official licensing and Copilot Credit guides before taking purchasing or enforcement action.

heading: 5. Export and Reconciliation
content: The Consumption Dashboard export is a public-preview feature for de-identified, user-level AI cost metrics. It is currently available to Insights Analysts with global partition access and Microsoft 365 Global Administrators . Manager export is not yet generally available in the 1 July 2026 source. The export does not include GitHub metrics. Export Window and freshness Use Export by week Complete data for the previous five months plus month-to-date data for the current month; activity is usually 2–8 days behind Weekly and monthly trend review or longer-term planning Export by day Start of the previous month through the export date; activity is usually up to two days behind Day-to-day cost review and emerging usage trends Only complete days or weeks are included. Each download is a ZIP containing two CSV files joined by PeopleHistoricalId : PersonServiceCreditsMetrics.csv — one row per person, service, and date, with hashed person ID, service, policy, sessions, limits, credits, licence status, and join key. PeopleMetaData.csv — one row per person, with the join key plus Organization and Job function where available. At the top right of the dashboard, select the download button. Se...

heading: 6. Success Criteria
content: Before operationalizing a recurring review, verify all of the following: The expected role can open the dashboard and sees only the documented company or group scope. A manager cannot switch to Company scope. A non-global partition analyst cannot open the dashboard or run consumption-metric queries. Covered Cowork or Work IQ API activity produces month-to-date users, sessions, and credits after processing. Organization and job-function filters return the expected population and change the totals coherently. Usage-intensity totals reconcile internally and do not exceed the selected population. Displayed spending-policy limits are confirmed with the policy owner. The export completes, carries the expected scope, and is stored under appropriate access controls. The official billing surface remains the source of record, with any discrepancy documented.

heading: Validation Failures
content: These errors occur before data is processed. The upload is rejected and no data is ingested. Error Cause Resolution Blank PersonEmail / PersonId One or more rows have an empty email field. Ensure every row has a valid email address in the PersonEmail (or PersonId / Microsoft_PersonEmail ) column. Missing required attributes CSV is missing one or more required columns: PersonEmail , ManagerEmail , Organization (or their Microsoft_* equivalents). Add the missing required columns to the CSV header row. See the Attribute Reference for the full mapping. Empty rows in CSV Blank rows (including trailing empty rows) cause validation to fail. Remove all empty rows from the CSV. Pay special attention to rows at the end of the file. Unlicensed apps referenced CSV references applications or services not licensed in the tenant. Remove or correct app references. Ensure all referenced services are active in the tenant. Unsupported custom attributes Custom attribute names contain spaces, special characters, or exceed 105 total attribute limit. Use underscores instead of spaces. Remove special characters. Reduce total attribute count to ≤105. Microsoft_ prefix on custom attributes Custom attribu...

heading: Processing Failures
content: Status Meaning Resolution Processing failed Backend error during data ingestion. Retry the upload. If the issue persists, contact Microsoft Support with the error details and correlation ID. Suspended Credentials expired or backend system unavailable (common with HRIS connectors). Re-authenticate the connector credentials. Retry the next day. If suspended repeatedly, check service health at status.office.com . Partial success Some rows failed validation (API, Workday, or SAP connectors only). Download the error report from the upload status page. Fix the failing rows and re-upload. Common causes: duplicate emails, malformed dates, invalid manager references. Validation Error Reference For the complete list of upload validation errors and error codes, see Validation errors for organizational data uploads on Microsoft Learn.

heading: Supported correction and rollback after access is restored
content: Use incremental CSV updates for affected users; omitted or blank values preserve existing data. Use documented type-specific deletion markers for values that must be removed: '' for strings in CSV, -1 for integers, a nonexistent in-tenant address for email, and 01-01-0001 for dates. Use Historical import with File valid as of for retroactive Viva Insights corrections. To return Viva ManagerId or Organization to Entra, enable those attributes under Configure Entra connection , then remove optional uploaded attributes no longer needed. Do not use a one-row dummy Replace All file as a general rollback. For critical migration failures, Microsoft documents temporary rollback only through the support channel in special cases. Sources: Import and manage your organizational data with a .csv file — Update or make other changes and Make retroactive updates (Microsoft Learn, updated 2 April 2026); Use Microsoft Entra plus .csv files for parallel data uploads — FAQs (visible date 30 June 2025; refreshed 7 July 2026); and Upload and maintain data through the Microsoft 365 admin center — Support and rollback (5 December 2025).

content: Message Center and the live tenant UI identify the supported add/edit surface. User signs into that surface and holds its matching directory-scoped role. doc: Scenario 02: Report Visible to One User Not Another
heading: 30-second diagnosis
content: Should the colleague see it at all? Check every access source in the matrix. If none applies, stop: this is working as designed. Which source grants your access? Confirm role, explicit assignment, Global partition, senior-leader eligibility, Manager Settings, or delegation. Do not infer viewing from AI Administrator or Insights Administrator. doc: Scenario 02: Report Visible to One User Not Another
heading: Worked example — the executive sponsor who isn't a manager
content: This case comes up constantly, so it's worth walking through end-to-end because the intuitive answer is the wrong one. The situation James is the executive sponsor of the organisation's Copilot rollout. He needs the org-wide adoption report to track the programme. He is not a people-manager — he has no direct reports — and he holds no admin role. The instinctive move is "he's an exec, just give him manager access." That doesn't work, and it's worth understanding why. Why "manager" is the wrong lever. In Viva Insights, "manager" is not a statement about seniority or importance — it is a literal test of whether the person has direct or indirect reports , established by the PersonId / ManagerId hierarchy. You cannot enable someone as a manager who has nobody reporting to them, no matter how senior they are. And even if James did have a small team, manager access would only show that team's adoption — not the company-wide view an executive sponsor actually needs. The correct lever here is delegation. James could receive company access through explicit assignment, qualifying senior-leader identification, Global-partition analyst assignment, Global Administrator, or an eligible compan...

doc: Scenario 02: Report Visible to One User Not Another
heading: Deliberately restricting access (the inverse problem)
content: The harder, higher-stakes version of this question is the reverse: "How do I make sure a particular leader or group cannot see the dashboard, even though their role or hierarchy would normally grant it?" This is the everyday reality in regulated, multi-agency, or shared-tenant deployments where one agency's leaders must not see another's data. Some access sources are automatic, including qualified senior-leader and enabled manager access. The VFAM gate is the supported surface-control lever for removing access : Goal How Watch out for Block a specific set of users from the dashboard Create a group-scoped VFAM policy that turns the Copilot Dashboard (and/or Viva Insights web app) feature OFF for an Entra group (e.g. AgencyUsers-NoDashboard ). Scope the policy to the group , never to "Everyone" — an Everyone-level block also strips access from non-agency executives who legitimately need it. Let agency managers see only their own agency Combine the VFAM block above with partition-based isolation so that, where access is granted, the data is already scoped to the agency. VFAM controls the surface ; partitions control the data . You usually need both for true isolation. Confirm a blo...

doc: Scenario 02: Report Visible to One User Not Another
heading: What "done" looks like
content: You can name the exact source that grants your access and scope, plus the required entitlement and absence of a VFAM block. You've confirmed the colleague either has no qualifying source — in which case it's correct by design — or you've found the missing item: explicit access, Global partition, eligible hierarchy, Manager Settings, delegation, entitlement, or VFAM. If the goal was the inverse — restricting access — the right users are blocked via a group-scoped VFAM policy (never "Everyone"), verified by a test user in the blocked group.

heading: 1. Glossary of Key Terms
content: Term Definition AI Administrator Entra ID role that manages applicable AI and Copilot Dashboard settings, access assignment, exclusions, and feature controls. It does not grant automatic Copilot Dashboard viewing or Insights Analyst query rights. Assisted Hours The estimated number of hours saved by employees using Copilot features. heading: 2. Requirements and Source Precedence
content: Surface / attribute Requirement or precedence Validation Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 Microsoft_PersonEmail is required. Additional attributes depend on the selected apps. Review the target-app selections and mapping before validation. Copilot Dashboard and Viva Insights Microsoft_PersonEmail , Microsoft_ManagerEmail , and Microsoft_Organization are required. heading: 3. Data Classification
content: When uploading organisational data, each attribute is assigned an access classification that controls which applications and roles can read the data. Classification Visibility Example Attributes Public Well-known organizational attributes made available to Microsoft 365 Profile and accessible to tenant users and Microsoft 365 apps, subject to the platform's app-sharing and precedence behavior. Organization , Location , LevelDesignation Confidential Sensitive well-known attributes for which the uploader chooses the apps that can receive the data. HourlyRate , CostCentre Custom Tenant-defined attributes with controls for choosing which apps can access the data. Any custom attribute where selective access is needed. Best Practice Set HourlyRate and any compensation-related attributes to Confidential . Set demographic attributes like Organization , Location , and FunctionType to Public so they are available as dashboard filters. Source: Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 (Microsoft Learn, updated 2 December 2025) defines public, confidential, and custom organizational data and app access controls.

heading: 4. Naming Conventions
content: Rule Details Use underscores, not spaces Column names must not contain spaces. Use Business_Unit or BusinessUnit , not Business Unit . No Microsoft_ prefix for custom attributes The Microsoft_ prefix is reserved for system attributes. Custom columns using this prefix will cause validation errors. Case sensitivity Attribute names are case-insensitive during upload but are stored as provided. Use consistent casing for clarity. Maximum length Attribute names should not exceed 128 characters. Total attribute limit Maximum of 105 total attributes per upload (reserved system attributes + your custom attributes combined). Avoid special characters Use only letters, numbers, and underscores. Avoid hyphens, periods, and other special characters in attribute names.

heading: 5. Reserved Attribute Names
content: The following attribute names are reserved by the system and cannot be used for custom attributes . If you need a similar concept, use a different name (e.g., Agency_Location instead of Location ). Reserved Name (Legacy) Reserved Name (M365 Admin Center) PersonId Microsoft_PersonEmail ManagerId Microsoft_ManagerEmail Organization Microsoft_Organization EffectiveDate N/A (UI-based) FunctionType Microsoft_JobDiscipline LevelDesignation Microsoft_LevelDesignation Location Microsoft_CompanyOfficeLocation HireDate Microsoft_HireDate HourlyRate Microsoft_HourlyRate Layer Microsoft_Layer SupervisorIndicator Microsoft_SupervisorIndicator Important Any custom attribute name beginning with Microsoft_ will be rejected during validation. If you need to add custom attributes with similar concepts, use your own naming convention (e.g., Agency , CostCentre , Region ).

heading: 6. Update, Delete, and Historical Correction
content: Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 uses incremental merge. Upload only affected users and include attributes you intend to update. An omitted or blank value preserves the existing value; use the documented marker when a value must be deleted. Third-party destinations, including Microsoft Learn and GitHub, have their own accessibility behavior and policies.

heading: Report an Accessibility Problem
content: Open an accessibility issue and include the page URL, browser, operating system, assistive technology, zoom level, steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and observed behavior. Do not include confidential or personal data. If an HTML page and its PDF differ, report both URLs and use the HTML page until the PDF is corrected.

Heading hierarchy, landmarks, link purpose, image alternatives, and table headers are checked. Generated PDFs pass structural preflight and manual screen-reader/reading-order spot checks. Incorrect, stale, missing, or overlapping Agency / Domain values can place people in the wrong query population or no population. Preview behavior, tenant rollout, service incidents, policy precedence, and product changes can invalidate a previously passing result.
