heading: 1. Executive Summary
content: This document provides an author-created, defense-in-depth reporting pattern for Microsoft Copilot Dashboard and Copilot Analytics in a single Microsoft 365 tenant that hosts multiple agencies . It coordinates product controls to reduce cross-agency reporting paths; it does not establish an end-to-end security boundary. Core Constraint & Key Finding: The design objective is to prevent routine agency-facing reporting paths from exposing another agency's data. The Copilot Dashboard and other tenant surfaces are governed separately from analyst partitions, and some access sources can remain tenant-wide. Therefore the objective must be expressed and tested per actor and surface rather than as an absolute guarantee. Recommended Approach: Two Coordinated Reporting Layers Viva Insights Advanced Analytics with Partitions — for row/column-scoped agency analyst query workspaces. Copilot Dashboard with controlled access — for tenant-level oversight by central IT only (secondary, restricted use). Power BI workspaces/apps — a separate agency-facing delivery layer with Viewer-first permissions, sharing/export controls, and optional RLS.

heading: 2.2 Functional Requirements
content: ID Requirement Priority FR-01 Agency-facing reports are limited to the approved agency population on every tested delivery path P0 — Must Have FR-02 Unapproved cross-agency access attempts fail in the mandatory actor/surface test matrix P0 — Must Have FR-03 Each agency CEO gets adoption, impact, and sentiment metrics P1 — Should Have FR-04 Central IT retains tenant-wide visibility for license management P1 — Should Have FR-05 Agency-level analysts can run custom Copilot queries P2 — Nice to Have

heading: 3. Solution Options Assessment
content: Option Description FR-01 (Agency Reporting) FR-02 (No Cross-Vis) Verdict 1. Dashboard Only Standard deployment relying on "Your group" scope. ❌ Fail ❌ Fail NOT RECOMMENDED. Senior leaders auto-get "Your company" view. 2. Dashboard + Delegation Admin delegates "Your group" view to CEOs. ❌ Fail ❌ Fail NOT RECOMMENDED. Scope is based on delegator's hierarchy. 3. Partitions (Advanced Analytics) Create Viva Insights Partitions per agency. ✅ Pass Partial — query workspace only RECOMMENDED COMPONENT. Limits rows and columns available to the assigned analyst in that partition; other surfaces require separate controls. 4. Hybrid (Partitions + Restricted Dashboard) Partitions for agencies + Dashboard for Central IT. ✅ Pass Conditional on all surface tests RECOMMENDED PATTERN. Eligible for go-live only after the evidence gate passes and residual risks are signed off.

heading: 4.1 Choose Your Path: CSV Org Data vs. Entra ID Domain
content: Viva Insights partitions filter on organizational data attributes . Attributes reach Viva Insights from two sources: Source Attributes Available Setup Required Microsoft Entra ID (automatic sync) PersonId , ManagerId , Organization (from Entra Department field), plus system-generated: Domain (from SMTP), TimeZone , PopulationType None — syncs automatically. Optionally enable ManagerId/Organization via "Configure Entra connection" CSV Org Data Upload All of the above, plus up to 105 custom attributes: Agency , FunctionType , Location , LevelDesignation , CostCentre , etc. Record current source precedence, then use Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 unless the tenant UI and Message Center explicitly expose a supported legacy path. Workday has a 100,000-user limit; API-based import is preview and environment-dependent.

heading: Path A: CSV Org Data Upload (Full Capability)
content: Use when: All agencies share the same email domain (e.g., everyone is @onetenant.gov.au ) You need both agency analyst query segmentation and department/location/job-function filtering You want the richest possible analytics (custom attributes, before/after cohort segmentation) HR can provide a consolidated employee CSV with an Agency column Partition filter: Agency = "Agency A" End-to-end workflow: Phase 0: Pre-flight audit (same as Path B) Phase 1: Lockdown — disable AutoCxO, delegation, Agent Dashboard, Metrics Export (same as Path B) Phase 2: Prepare and upload CSV with PersonId , ManagerId , Organization , EffectiveDate , and custom Agency attribute. Verify 100% coverage on Data Quality page. Phase 3: Create partitions filtered by Agency = "Agency A" , etc. Assign analysts. Phase 4: Analysts run Copilot PBI queries within their partition → publish reports. Time estimate: 2–3 days. Ongoing: Monthly CSV re-upload to keep data current.

heading: Path B: Entra ID Domain Filter (Zero CSV)
content: Use when: Each agency has a distinct email domain (e.g., agencya.gov.au , agencyb.gov.au ) All employees use their agency domain as their primary SMTP address You do not need custom attribute filtering (Department/Location come only from Entra defaults) You want the fastest possible deployment with zero data preparation Partition filter: Domain = "agencya.gov.au" End-to-end workflow: Phase 0: Pre-flight audit (same as Path A) Phase 1: Lockdown — disable AutoCxO, delegation, Agent Dashboard, Metrics Export (same as Path A) Phase 2: Skip CSV upload. Optionally enable ManagerId and Organization in "Configure Entra connection" for richer hierarchy and department data. Phase 3: Create partitions filtered by Domain = "agencya.gov.au" , etc. Assign analysts. Phase 4: Analysts run Copilot PBI queries within their partition → publish reports. Time estimate: 1 day (no CSV preparation for the partition filter). Ongoing: Monitor primary SMTP domains, transfers, exceptions, contractors, and shared-service identities; verify partition counts after changes.

heading: Decision Matrix
content: Question If Yes → Path Do all agencies share the same email domain? Path A (CSV required — Domain cannot differentiate agencies) Does each agency have a unique email domain? Path B is available (can also use Path A for richer attributes) Do you need Department/Location/Job Function filters per agency? Path A (CSV adds these attributes beyond Entra defaults) Are there shared-services staff on a common domain? Path A preferred (assign Agency = "Shared Services" in CSV) Need the fastest deployment with minimal data preparation? Path B (zero CSV, Domain-only filter) Want to start with Path B and add CSV later? Path B now, Path A later — you can always upload CSV to add richer attributes. Use the parallel data ingestion feature to keep Entra as source for ManagerId/Organization while adding custom attributes via CSV. Important: Whichever path you choose, the group-scoped surface restrictions , partition assignment governance, Power BI controls, and evidence gate are the same. The path changes how organizational data reaches Viva Insights and which attribute scopes the analyst query workspace.

heading: 4.2 Entra ID Attributes Reference for Viva Insights
content: Understanding exactly which Entra ID attributes reach Viva Insights is critical for choosing the right path. Per Microsoft Learn : Already know your path? If you have already decided between Path A and Path B in Section 4.1 , you can skip this reference section and go directly to the Implementation Runbook (Section 5) .

heading: Attributes Synced from Entra ID
content: Entra ID Source Field Viva Insights Attribute Available for Partition Filter? Notes UserPrincipalName PersonId No (identifier only) Always synced Manager / UserPrincipalName ManagerId Yes (required attribute) Enable via "Configure Entra connection" checkbox Department Organization Yes (required attribute) Enable via "Configure Entra connection" checkbox

heading: System-Generated Attributes (Always Available)
content: Attribute Source Available for Partition Filter? Multi-Agency Use Domain Primary SMTP address Yes Key attribute for Path B — if each agency has a distinct email domain, this is your partition filter TimeZone Outlook/Exchange settings Yes Useful for geography analysis; do not treat it as a reliable agency identifier without validation PopulationType SMTP address Yes Distinguishes internal vs. external collaborators

heading: Attributes NOT Available from Entra ID
content: The following Entra ID user profile fields are not synced to Viva Insights. If you need them, you must upload via CSV: On-Premises Active Directory Pipeline: Do not assume custom on-premises or Entra extension attributes flow through the Viva Entra connector. Verify the actual available attributes in Data Quality/Profile. Supply required custom attributes through a currently supported organizational-data import path, which may be CSV, a supported HR connector, or API-based import preview depending on cloud and tenant state. Entra ID Field Status in Viva Insights Company Not synced — use CSV Organization or custom attribute Division Not synced JobTitle Not synced — use CSV FunctionType City / Country / State Not synced — use CSV Location / CountryOrRegion EmployeeType Not synced extensionAttribute1-15 Not synced Common Misconception: Do not assume Company , Division , or extension attributes automatically appear in Viva Insights. Confirm connector output and attribute precedence in Data Quality/Profile; supply missing attributes through the supported tenant import path rather than assuming CSV is the only option.

heading: Parallel Data Ingestion (Best of Both Worlds)
content: You can use both Entra ID and CSV simultaneously via the parallel data ingestion feature: Let Entra continue providing ManagerId and Organization (always accurate, auto-refreshing) Supply only the additional attributes you need (for example, Agency , Location , CostCentre ) through the currently supported import path Entra-sourced attributes take precedence for the fields you select; CSV provides the rest This is ideal for organisations starting with Path B (Domain-based) who later want to add richer attributes without disrupting existing Entra-based data flows.

heading: 0 Phase 0: Pre-Flight Audit
content: Before locking anything down, audit the current state and obtain approvals. Disabling policies does not revoke existing access. Pre-flight approvals (complete before proceeding): Obtain written Security, Compliance/Legal, Privacy/DPO, Viva Insights, and Power BI owner approval of the control design, test plan, accepted global access, residual risks, rollback, and evidence requirements. Do not ask reviewers to attest that partitions alone satisfy a regulation. Confirm all agency heads and compliance officers agree on the minimum group size threshold (see Phase 3 ). Document the agreed number. Confirm role assignments are in place: AI Admin and Insights Admin roles assigned to Central IT staff via M365 Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Add-on Plan, or via Role assignments → search role name. ( Learn reference ) Audit VFAM policies (Admin UI): Navigate to admin.microsoft.com → Settings → Microsoft Viva → Viva Insights → Set up and management → Turn Advanced Insights on or off . Review the status of each feature: AutoCxoIdentification, CopilotDashboardDelegation, AgentDashboard, CopilotMetricsReport. Document the current state (On/Off, scope: Everyone or Specific user...

heading: 1 Phase 1: Group-Scoped Surface Restrictions
content: Reduce direct agency-user access to tenant-level Viva Insights surfaces. Complete each sub-step in order and verify effective access after propagation. This layer does not alter global, analyst-query, downloaded-result, or Power BI permissions. Requires AI Administrator role. Group-Scoped Policies — Protect Non-Agency Leaders VFAM policies set to "Everyone" affect the entire tenant — including non-agency executives (CFO, CISO, CIO) who may legitimately need dashboard access. To avoid this, create an Entra security group called AgencyUsers-NoDashboard containing all agency employees. Target your disable policies at this group instead of Everyone. This preserves access for Central IT, Finance, and other non-agency stakeholders. All steps below use group-scoped targeting. See Appendix A for the full PowerShell reference. 1.1 — Disable Auto CxO Identification Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Turn Advanced Insights on or off → select Auto CxO Identification → Add policy → scope to AgencyUsers-NoDashboard → set to Off → Save. 1.2 — Remove existing auto-enabled agency leaders Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Manage user access for Copilot Dashboard → rem...

heading: 2 Phase 2: Organizational Data
content: Prepare the foundational data that enables partitioning. Your approach depends on which path you chose in Section 4.1 . For detailed refresh cadence tables, see Section 6: Organizational Data Strategy .

heading: Path A: CSV Org Data Upload
content: Create a consolidated CSV file containing ALL employees across ALL agencies. Include the critical custom attribute: Agency (e.g., "Agency A", "Agency B"). Include required fields: Microsoft_PersonEmail , Microsoft_ManagerEmail , Microsoft_Organization (M365 Admin Center names) or PersonId , ManagerId , Organization , EffectiveDate (legacy Viva Insights names). Before upload, record attribute source/precedence and check Setup → Migration and imports , Message Center, and tenant UI. Use Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 for new/edited uploads unless the tenant explicitly presents a supported legacy path. Workday has a documented 100,000-user limit; API-based import remains preview and requires an environment check. Path A Verification: Check Viva Insights Admin → Data Quality to verify the Agency attribute has 100% coverage. Verify employee counts per agency match HR records.

heading: Path B: Entra ID Domain (No CSV)
content: No CSV upload required. The Domain attribute is system-generated from each employee's primary SMTP address. Recommended: Navigate to Viva Insights → Organizational data → Configure Entra connection → enable ManagerId and Organization checkboxes → Save . This enriches your data with manager hierarchy and department info (takes ~24 hours to sync). Verify domain mapping: confirm that each agency's employees use a distinct email domain (e.g., agencya.gov.au , agencyb.gov.au ). Path B Verification: In Viva Insights Admin → Organizational data, verify that Domain appears as an available attribute. Check the Data Quality page to see Domain values and confirm they cleanly map to agencies. If any employees use a shared domain (e.g., contractors on shared.gov.au ), decide how to handle them (see Shared Services Decision below). Shared Services Decision (both paths): If your organisation has shared-services teams (IT, Legal, Finance) that serve multiple agencies, choose one of these approaches: Dedicated "Shared Services" partition (recommended) — Create a separate partition filtered by Agency = "Shared Services" (Path A) or Domain = "shared.gov.au" (Path B). Central IT analysts access thi...

heading: 3 Phase 3: Partitions Setup
content: Create the row/column controls for each agency analyst query workspace. The partition filter differs by path, but all other steps are identical. These controls do not scope the other surfaces in the boundary matrix. Click the diagram to enlarge · use + / − to zoom · press Esc to close Figure 1 — Where partition enabling fits. Phase 3 has two actions that are easy to conflate: Enable Partitions () is a one-time tenant-wide switch, while New partition () is repeated per agency. Assigning each analyst to the intended partition only () is required for that analyst query workspace to remain scoped. It does not govern the other surfaces in this pattern. Each numbered step links to its Microsoft Learn reference: VFAM lockdown of the Copilot Dashboard — Viva feature access management What gets locked down (the tenant-wide view) — Connect to the Copilot Dashboard Upload org data carrying the Agency / Domain attribute — Organizational data in Microsoft 365 Enable Partitions — the one-time tenant switch — Customize privacy settings → Partitions Create one partition per agency and set its filter — Partitions in Viva Insights Assign the Insights A...

heading: 4 Phase 4: Copilot Reporting
content: Generate partition-scoped query outputs and distribute reports through a separately governed Power BI or approved manager-reporting path.

heading: 4a — What Copilot Analytics Are Available in a Partition?
content: Not all Copilot analytics surfaces are partition-scoped. Use this matrix to set expectations with agency stakeholders: Analytics Surface Available in Agency Partition? Notes Custom Person Queries (Copilot metrics) YES — partition-scoped query workspace Expected rows and columns follow the selected partition. Verify population and negative-test other assignments. Power BI Templates (Copilot Adoption, Impact Explorer, Business Impact, Headlines, etc.) YES — query starts in the partition Verify the downloaded result before publishing; Power BI permissions and export are independent controls. Custom Metrics & Metric Rules YES — created in the selected partition Verify visibility with an analyst from another partition; do not infer end-to-end isolation. Analysis Results YES — associated with the selected partition Test result-list visibility and download with same-partition, other-partition, multi-partition, and global analysts. Copilot Dashboard NO — not partition-scoped Separately governed by access sources, hierarchy, and group-scoped policies; pattern target is approved Central IT access. Copilot Analytics Reports (OOB reports) NO — not governed by the agency partition alone Test...

heading: 4b — Deliver Reports to Agency Heads
content: Agency analysts run Copilot Power BI template queries within their assigned partition and validate the result population before publication. Option A: Power BI Workspaces (requires Power BI Pro/Premium licences for viewers) Create dedicated Power BI workspaces per agency (e.g., Agency-A-Copilot-Analytics ). Publish reports and grant Viewer access to agency leadership via Entra group. Configure Row-Level Security (RLS) as a secondary control if multiple agencies share a workspace. Licensing: Each viewer needs a Power BI Pro or Premium Per User (PPU) licence. Alternatively, if your organisation has a Power BI Premium capacity (P or F SKU), reports published to that capacity can be viewed by users with a free Power BI licence — eliminating per-user viewer costs. Consult your licensing/finance owner. Option B: Viva Insights Publish Reports (prerequisites apply — review notes below) Use the Publish Reports feature in the Viva Insights analyst experience. "Filter report by manager hierarchy": Publishes to enabled managers . The intended report follows the recipient's hierarchy, but shared executives, cross-agency managers, source errors, and other access paths require direct testing b...

heading: 6. Organizational Data Strategy
content: How employee data reaches Viva Insights depends on the chosen path and current tenant migration state. Before every change, record attribute-level source and precedence, inspect Data Quality and Profile, check Setup → Migration and imports plus Message Center, and use the path actually supported in the tenant. Do not infer that one upload universally replaces Entra.

heading: 6.1 Path A: CSV Org Data Upload
content: Data Type Source Refresh Cadence Employee hierarchy (ManagerId) Centralized HR or per-agency HR feeds (CSV), HRIS connector, or API-based import Monthly (minimum). HRIS connectors support weekly auto-sync. Custom attributes (Agency, FunctionType) HR system (via CSV, HRIS connector, or API) Monthly. If using API-based import, incremental refresh is supported — add new hires without re-uploading all employees. Copilot usage data Automatic from M365 signals Daily (6-day delay) Data Quality Rule: The Agency attribute must have 100% coverage. Missing values will exclude employees from all partitions. Data Ownership Model for Multi-Agency CSV Management Designate one Central IT owner responsible for the consolidated CSV file across all agencies. Establish a monthly collection process: Each agency HR team exports their employee data extract → Central IT merges all extracts into a single CSV with the Agency attribute populated for every row. Validate before upload: Check that every row has a non-empty Agency value, that employee counts per agency match HR records, and that the PersonId (email) format is correct. Upload and verify: Use Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 for new/edited ...

heading: 6.2 Path B: Entra ID Domain (No CSV)
content: Data Type Source Refresh Cadence Domain (agency identifier) Primary SMTP address (system-generated) Automatic — updates as employees are added/removed in Entra Employee hierarchy (ManagerId) Entra ID (enable via Configure Entra connection) Automatic — ~24-hour sync Department (Organization) Entra ID Department field (enable via Configure Entra connection) Automatic — ~24-hour sync Copilot usage data Automatic from M365 signals Daily (6-day delay) Key advantage: No ongoing data maintenance. New employees automatically appear in the correct partition based on their SMTP domain. Departing employees are automatically removed when their account is disabled. Path B Limitation: You cannot filter by custom attributes (Cost Centre, Job Function, Location, etc.) within agency partitions. If you need these filters, upload a CSV using the parallel data ingestion method — Entra continues providing ManagerId and Organization while the CSV adds custom attributes.

heading: 6.3 Upgrading from Path B to Path A
content: Organisations can start with Path B for rapid deployment and later add a CSV upload to unlock richer analytics: Enable parallel data ingestion in Viva Insights → Organizational data → Configure Entra connection. Keep Entra as the source for ManagerId and Organization . Upload a CSV with additional attributes ( Agency , FunctionType , Location , etc.). Update partition filters from Domain to Agency if preferred (edit each partition → change filter condition).

heading: 8. Governance & Ongoing Operations
content: Monthly (Path A): Refresh organizational data through the supported path and verify source, precedence, coverage, and partition counts. Path B: no CSV refresh, but verify domain mapping, exceptions, transfers, and shared-service identities. Monthly: Refresh Copilot PBI template queries and publish reports (Agency Analysts). Quarterly: Re-run every actor/surface negative test; recertify global and multi-partition access, shared executives, cross-agency managers, delegates, conflicts of interest, Power BI roles, and export/resharing settings; retain the evidence pack. Onboarding: To add a new agency: Path A — add them to the CSV, create a new partition, assign an analyst. Path B — create a partition filtered by the new agency's domain, assign an analyst. Both paths: spin up a new PBI workspace or use Publish Reports. Every material change: follow change control, capture the pre-change state, pilot, retest, obtain approvals, and veto continued publication on failure.

heading: 10. Discovery Questions for Customer
content: Is the requirement reporting segmentation or a tenant/cryptographic/sovereign/certified security boundary? This pattern addresses only the former. Escalate the latter to Security architecture, Compliance/Privacy/DPO, and Microsoft Support. Does each agency have a distinct email domain, or do all agencies share one domain? This determines the candidate analyst-partition attribute, not an end-to-end boundary. See Section 4.1 . How is the Entra ID ManagerID hierarchy currently structured? (Determines if CEOs are auto-identified as senior leaders). How many total employees per agency, and how many have Copilot licenses? (Partitions need sufficient population to meet the minimum group size threshold, default 10). Are there shared-services teams (IT, Legal, Finance) that serve multiple agencies? heading: Copilot Analytics with Agency-Default Views for Multi-Agency Single Tenant
content: Version: 1.1 | Author: Sumit Sadhu | Last validated: 16 July 2026 THIS GUIDE'S AUTHOR-CREATED ARCHITECTURE PATTERN Default Priority is an editorial name created by this guide. It is not a Microsoft product, security, compliance, sovereignty, or licensing term. The companion Restricted Reporting pattern was formerly labelled "Isolation"; the old word remains in the existing filename and URL only for link stability. Microsoft documents the component capabilities, but does not document either combined pattern as an end-to-end security boundary. This pattern intentionally accepts that some authorized users can obtain broader cross-agency or tenant-wide views. Manager hierarchy can establish a useful default scope, but it is not an authorization boundary. Viva Insights partitions limit rows and columns in an assigned analyst query workspace; separate controls govern Dashboard, Analytics Reports, Agent Dashboard, metrics export, delegation, manager access, privileged roles, downloaded results, and Power BI. Neither pattern creates cryptographic, tenant, product-security, or independently certified isolation.

heading: Pattern Boundary: Surface by Actor
content: Actor / surface What controls it Residual risk and required action Agency leader or manager — Copilot Dashboard / Analytics Reports Access source plus manager hierarchy A shared executive or cross-agency manager can span agencies; a tenant-wide access source can expose "Your company". Confirm that this is accepted and test each account. Delegate — Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Copilot insights Delegator eligibility and scope The delegate receives the delegator's scope. Audit creation, inherited scope, and removal. See Delegate access to the Copilot Dashboard, Agent Dashboard, and Copilot insights (last updated 9 February 2026). Agency analyst — advanced query workspace Insights Analyst role plus partition assignment Multiple or global partition assignments broaden query data. Record business purpose and conflict-of-interest approval for cross-agency analysts. Global Administrator, globally assigned user, or global-partition analyst Privileged assignment and least-privilege governance Tenant-wide visibility remains by design. Record owner, purpose, expiry, and quarterly recertification. Insights Administrator and AI Administrator roles do not by themselves grant every view; rol...

heading: Mandatory Negative Tests, Evidence, and Release Veto
content: Test agency leader, cross-agency manager, shared executive, delegate, agency analyst, multi-partition analyst, global-partition analyst, Power BI Viewer and Member/Contributor, AI Administrator, Insights Administrator, Insights Analyst, and Global Administrator accounts. Test Copilot Dashboard, Analytics Reports, Agent Dashboard, weekly/daily metrics export, advanced query workspace, query-result download, delegation, manager-published reports, Power BI workspace/app, report export, Analyze in Excel, subscriptions, and resharing. Capture test date, tester identity/role, group and partition memberships, policy/access export, org-data snapshot, expected result, observed result, screenshot or audit record, Power BI permissions, owner, and remediation ticket. Any failed or unexecutable negative test vetoes publication and go-live. Restore the pre-change state or remove access, preserve evidence, and open Microsoft Support when documented and observed behavior differ. Obtain written Security, Compliance/Legal, Privacy/DPO, Viva Insights owner, and Power BI owner sign-off on accepted visibility and residual risk. Repeat the suite quarterly and after any hierarchy, role, delegation, or...

heading: Which Guide Do I Need?
content: There are two author-created reporting-pattern guides . Choose based on your accepted visibility and tested controls: You are here Default-Priority (Open) Agencies see their own data by default; cross-agency visibility is acceptable Go to Restricted Reporting Pattern Defense-in-depth reporting segmentation; legacy URL retained Dimension This Guide (Default Priority) Restricted Reporting Pattern Core posture Open — agencies default to their own data but authorized users may see aggregate tenant data Restricted — partition-scoped analyst queries plus separate surface and delivery controls VFAM features AutoCxO and delegation enabled for approved users; Agent Dashboard only where preview eligibility, cloud support, and privacy review pass Group-scoped restrictions target direct agency access; effective policy is tested per feature Copilot Dashboard Available to approved agency leaders; expected starting scope is hierarchy-derived and must be tested Pattern target is approved Central IT access; every other access source is separately tested Partitions Optional — for row/column-scoped analyst query workspaces Required for agency analyst query workspaces; not a control for other surfa...

heading: 1. Executive Summary
content: This document provides an author-created reporting pattern for a single Microsoft 365 tenant that hosts multiple agencies , where each agency wants to see its own Copilot adoption and impact data by default and explicitly accepts broader authorized cross-agency or tenant-wide visibility. Key Difference from Restricted Reporting: Restricted Reporting applies group-scoped restrictions and partitioned analyst workspaces to reduce cross-agency reporting paths. This guide keeps eligible native features available because broader authorized visibility is accepted. Hierarchy provides a default view, not a security boundary. Recommended Approach: Native Scoping + Optional Partitions Copilot Dashboard with "Your group" default — agency leaders auto-identified; see their own hierarchy's data by default. Dashboard Delegation — CEOs delegate down to VPs and directors for cascading agency-scoped views. Publish Reports — filtered by manager hierarchy for automatic agency-scoped delivery. Partitions (optional) — for analyst-level deep dives scoped to a single agency; analysts may access multiple partitions if collaboration requires it.

heading: 2.2 Functional Requirements
content: ID Requirement Priority FR-01 Agency leaders see their own agency's Copilot data by default when opening the dashboard P0 — Must Have FR-02 Agency leaders can delegate dashboard views to their direct reports P0 — Must Have FR-03 Each agency CEO gets adoption, impact, and sentiment metrics for their agency P1 — Should Have FR-04 Central IT retains tenant-wide visibility for licence management and governance P1 — Should Have FR-05 Agency-level analysts can run custom Copilot queries scoped to their agency P2 — Nice to Have FR-06 Cross-agency aggregate visibility (e.g., "Your company" toggle) is acceptable if available P2 — Acceptable FR-07 CIO / Secretary-General gets a consolidated cross-agency view comparing adoption, impact, and ROI across all agencies P1 — Should Have Key distinction from Restricted Reporting: FR-06 explicitly accepts broader authorized visibility. The companion pattern instead treats cross-agency reporting access as a risk to reduce and test surface by surface; it still does not constitute tenant or security isolation.

heading: 3. Solution Options Assessment
content: Option Description FR-01 (Default View) FR-02 (Delegation) Verdict 1. Dashboard + AutoCxO + Delegation Enable native features. CEOs auto-get "Your group" as their default scope. Pass Pass RECOMMENDED for most scenarios. Zero friction, zero lockdown. 2. Option 1 + Partitions for Analysts Add partitions for analyst-level scoped queries alongside native dashboard. Pass Pass RECOMMENDED ARCHITECTURE. Best of both worlds. 3. Restricted Reporting (Partitions + Surface Controls) Restrict direct agency access to selected native surfaces and use partition-scoped queries plus separately governed report delivery. Pass Overkill NOT REQUIRED for this accepted-visibility posture. See the Restricted Reporting guide when stronger segmentation is needed.

heading: How "Your Group" Scoping Works
content: The Copilot Dashboard identifies senior leaders using the Entra ID manager hierarchy ( Auto CxO Identification ). Automatic identification applies only to tenants with at least 2,500 Entra ID users and a majority single reporting line; within that, a separate agency's top leader is auto-identified only if that agency tree is at least 4% of the total tenant . Smaller tenants or sub-4% agencies must grant the CEO access manually — the "Your group" scope still works once access is granted. Leaders with access see two scopes: Scope What It Shows Default? "Your group" Copilot data for all employees in the leader's management chain Yes — lands on this scope by default when opening the dashboard "Your company" Tenant-wide Copilot data across all agencies No — available via toggle, but not the default view When each agency CEO is the top of their hierarchy, "Your group" = their entire agency . The dashboard opens to this view by default, immediately satisfying the "see my agency data first" requirement without any lockdown. What about "Your company"? Agency leaders with a tenant-wide access source may be able to use "Your company" and see aggregate data across agencies. This pattern exp...

heading: 4.1 Manager Hierarchy Setup
content: The entire default-priority architecture depends on a clean manager hierarchy in Entra ID. Each agency must form a self-contained tree.

heading: Hierarchy Requirements
content: Requirement Why It Matters How to Verify Each agency CEO has no manager set in Entra, or reports to a tenant-level admin outside all agencies If CEO-A reports to CEO-B, then CEO-B's "Your group" will include Agency A employees Entra portal → Users → select CEO → Properties → Manager field All agency employees have a manager chain that terminates at their agency CEO Broken chains (null ManagerID mid-tree) cause employees to not appear in "Your group" Run the PowerShell audit in Appendix A Shared-services staff report through a clear chain If shared services staff report to an agency CEO, they appear in that agency's "Your group." If they report to a separate Shared Services head, they appear only in the company view. Not desired. Common Pitfall: In so...

heading: 4.2 Enrich with Organizational Data (Optional)
content: Org data enrichment adds filter dimensions within the dashboard and analytics views. This is not required for agency-default scoping (the hierarchy handles that), but enhances the analytics experience. Enrichment Method What It Adds When to Use Entra ID Attributes (zero effort) Organization (Department), ManagerId , Domain , TimeZone Always enable. Navigate to Viva Insights → Organizational data → Configure Entra connection → enable checkboxes. CSV Org Data Upload Custom attributes: Agency , FunctionType , Location , LevelDesignation , CostCentre , etc. When you need filters beyond Entra defaults. Record attribute precedence and use Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 unless the tenant UI and Message Center explicitly expose a supported legacy path. HRIS Connectors (SAP SuccessFactors, Workday) Same attributes as CSV, pulled directly from source HRIS. Auto-sync weekly or monthly. When your organisation uses SAP SuccessFactors or Workday and wants to eliminate manual CSV preparation. SAP SF | Workday API-Based Import (Preview) Same attributes as CSV, pushed programmatically via REST API. When you have a custom HRIS or data warehouse and want automated ingestion with incremental ...

heading: 0 Phase 0: Pre-Flight Verification
content: Audit the current state before configuring anything. Verify manager hierarchy: Confirm each agency CEO has no cross-agency manager relationship. Use Entra portal → Users → select each CEO → check Manager field. Run the PowerShell audit in Appendix A for bulk verification. Confirm assigned service plans: Apply the conflict-safe licensing gate in Pattern Boundary & Release Gates . Verify feature presence after processing; do not infer failure from a mixed licence assignment. Confirm supported environment: Record the exact cloud and verify Dashboard, Agent, export, Power BI templates, and organizational-data path in the tenant UI and Message Center. Identify agency leaders: List each agency's CEO and their direct reports (VPs). These will be the primary dashboard consumers and delegation targets. Review current VFAM state: Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Turn Advanced Insights on or off. Document which features are currently enabled or disabled. Check existing dashboard access: Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Manage user access for Copilot Dashboard. Note who currently has access. Capture rollback baseline: Export policies, access, delegations, rol...

heading: 1 Phase 1: Enable Native Features
content: Enable only the eligible Dashboard features approved for this accepted-visibility posture. Agent Dashboard and metrics export require separate preview, cloud, privacy, and access decisions. 1.1 — Enable Auto CxO Identification Admin Center → Settings → Viva → Viva Insights → Turn Advanced Insights on or off → Auto CxO Identification → ensure set to On for Everyone (or verify no disable policy exists). This automatically identifies agency CEOs (and other senior leaders) based on the Entra ID hierarchy and grants them Copilot Dashboard access with the "Your group" scope. 1.2 — Enable Dashboard Delegation Same page → Copilot Dashboard Delegation → ensure set to On for Everyone . This allows agency CEOs to delegate their dashboard view to their direct reports (VPs, directors). Delegates see the same scope as their delegator — the agency's data. 1.3 — Enable Agent Dashboard Only if the environment and tenant pass the gate: Agent Dashboard is preview. Treat 50 Microsoft 365 Copilot licences plus agent activity as the conservative prerequisite, validate availability in Worldwide, and do not expect it in national or regional clouds. Scope access to the approved population; do not defaul...

heading: 2 Phase 2: Configure Delegation & Manager Settings
content: Set up the delegation chain so agency leaders can share their dashboard view with their teams. 2.1 — Agency CEO Delegates to VPs Each agency CEO should: Navigate to insights.cloud.microsoft → Copilot Dashboard. Select the Share or Delegate option (top-right corner of the dashboard). Add their direct reports (VPs, directors) as delegates. Delegates will receive access and see the same agency-scoped view ("Your group" inheriting the CEO's scope). 2.2 — Enable Manager Settings for Publish Reports Navigate to insights.cloud.microsoft → Admin → Manager settings . Enable managers to receive published reports. Set the minimum team size threshold (this is the same tenant-wide privacy setting). Agency CEOs with team sizes above the threshold will be eligible to receive published reports filtered to their hierarchy. 2.3 — Enrich Organizational Data (Optional) Navigate to Viva Insights → Organizational data → Configure Entra connection → enable ManagerId and Organization checkboxes → Save. If you need custom attributes, first record current source precedence and confirm the tenant-supported upload path; then prepare the data per Appendix B . Uploaded attributes enrich filters but do not go...

heading: 3 Phase 3: Publish Reports
content: Use Viva Insights Publish Reports to deliver curated analytics to agency leaders automatically. 3.1 — Run Copilot PBI Template Queries Insights analysts (Central IT or agency-level) navigate to insights.cloud.microsoft → Create analysis. Select a Copilot Power BI template (Copilot Adoption, Impact Explorer, Business Impact, etc.). Run the query. If partitions are enabled, run within the appropriate partition. If not, run against the global population. 3.2 — Publish with "Filter by Manager Hierarchy" After the query completes, select Publish report . Choose "Filter report by manager hierarchy" . Each enabled manager (CEO, VP) will receive a version of the report filtered to their team. Set an expiry date (up to 365 days) and republish monthly to keep data current. Why this is eligible in Default Priority: This pattern accepts tenant-wide visibility from documented access sources. Manager publishing is still a separate surface: pilot a shared executive and cross-agency manager, verify Dashboard and report scope, capture evidence, and roll back manager enablement if observed access exceeds the approved posture.

heading: 4 Phase 4: Partitions for Analyst Deep Dives (Optional — Mandatory for CIO Reporting)
content: If your organisation has designated analysts per agency who need to run custom queries, partitions provide row/column-scoped analyst workspaces. This phase is optional for agency-level reporting (dashboard views and published reports may suffice), but needed for the cross-agency query approach described for CIO reporting. A global partition is tenant-wide and requires explicit conflict-of-interest and least-privilege approval. See Section 6.2 . Before Enabling Partitions: (a) Understand that enabling partitions is irreversible without contacting Microsoft support and will delete all partition configurations if disabled. (b) Export all existing query results before enabling partitions. (c) Partitions are needed when analysts require row/column-scoped custom query workspaces. Dashboard and manager-published views may follow hierarchy, but their actual scope must be tested separately. Navigate to insights.cloud.microsoft → Admin → Privacy settings → Partitions → Enable Partitions. Create a partition per agency (e.g., "Agency A"), filtered by Agency attribute (CSV) or Domain (Entra). Assign agency analysts to their primary partition. Cross-agency access: Analysts may be assigned to ...

heading: 6. Analytics Surfaces per Role
content: Default Priority can keep eligible analytics surfaces available to approved users. Availability, scope, preview status, cloud support, delegation, and export must be evaluated separately; hierarchy supplies a default dashboard view, not a universal access boundary. Analytics Surface Agency CEO / VP Agency Analyst (with partition) Central IT Copilot Dashboard — "Your group" Default view = their agency — Default view = their reports Copilot Dashboard — "Your company" Available via toggle (acceptable) — Full tenant view Copilot Analytics Reports (OOB) Via Dashboard access Via global or partition Full access Agent Dashboard Preview; tenant-wide scope where eligible and approved — Preview; validate conservative prerequisite, agent activity, cloud, and access Publish Reports (manager-filtered) Filtered to their hierarchy — Can publish Custom Person Queries — Scoped to assigned partition(s) Global or any partition Power BI Templates — Scoped to assigned partition(s) Global or any partition Dashboard Delegation Can delegate to direct reports — Can delegate Copilot Metrics Export Preview; only if separately eligible, approved, and enabled — Preview; global-access and privacy controls app...

heading: 6.1 ROI Analysis per Agency: Capabilities & Gaps
content: Agency leaders asking "What is Copilot's ROI for my agency?" will find that some metrics scope naturally to their hierarchy and some do not. Set expectations upfront using the matrix below.

heading: Which ROI Metrics Are Agency-Scoped?
content: ROI Metric Scoped to "Your group" (Agency)? Notes Copilot Assisted Hours Yes Impact page shows Assisted Hours for the leader's management hierarchy. Agency CEOs see their agency's total. Copilot Assisted Value Yes Calculated as Assisted Hours × Hourly Rate. Scoped to "Your group." However , the hourly rate is a single tenant-wide setting (see limitation below). Adoption metrics (active users, usage intensity, consistent usage) Yes Adoption page filters to "Your group" by default. Sentiment (Glint / Pulse / CSV survey) No — tenant-level only Sentiment page on the Copilot Dashboard shows tenant-wide data only. Not filterable by "Your group." Agency leaders see all agencies' survey responses. Agent usage (Agent Dashboard) No — "Your company" only Agent Dashboard has no "Your group" scope. Agency leaders see tenant-wide agent adoption. Licence cost data Not in dashboard Licence costs are in M365 Admin Center / billing. Not connected to the Copilot Dashboard. See the Lifecycle & Billing Playbook for cost-side data. Limitation: Single Tenant-Wide Hourly Rate Copilot Assisted Value is calculated as Assisted Hours × Hourly Rate . The hourly rate defaults to $72/hour and can be changed o...

heading: 6.2 Cross-Agency Impact & Adoption Analysis (CIO View)
content: A CIO or Secretary-General sitting above all agencies needs a consolidated cross-agency view that the Copilot Dashboard does not natively provide. The dashboard shows either "Your group" (one agency) or "Your company" (blended tenant aggregate) — neither provides side-by-side agency comparison.

heading: What the CIO Needs vs. What the Dashboard Provides
content: CIO Need Dashboard Support Solution Cross-agency side-by-side comparison None Global partition analyst runs Copilot person query grouped by Agency attribute → builds cross-agency PBI report Feature adoption depth per agency None (per-app breakdown exists but not compared across groups) M365 Copilot Adoption PBI template via global partition, grouped by Agency Behavioral impact per agency (meeting time, focus time changes) None Custom person queries with Copilot + collaboration metrics, grouped by Agency Consolidated executive roll-up report None Custom PBI dashboard: agency scorecards ranked by adoption %, Assisted Hours, feature depth Cross-agency benchmarking None PBI with internal percentile calculations per agency CIO Reporting Architecture Phase 4 (partitions) is mandatory for CIO reporting — the global partition is the CIO's primary data source. Without partitions, cross-agency comparison is impossible. Designate a Central IT analyst on the global partition as the CIO's primary reporting resource. This analyst runs queries across all employees, grouped by the Agency org data attribute. Recommended PBI executive dashboard structure: Page 1 — Agency Scorecard: Ranked table o...

heading: 10. Discovery Questions for Customer
content: Which cross-agency and tenant-wide views are explicitly acceptable, by actor and surface? If the requirement is tenant, cryptographic, sovereign, or certified security isolation, stop and obtain a security architecture assessment; Restricted Reporting is only defense-in-depth reporting segmentation. How is the Entra ID manager hierarchy structured? Does each agency CEO sit at the top of an independent tree, or is there a shared parent? How many agencies, and how many employees per agency? (Each agency needs enough employees to meet the minimum group size threshold for "Your group" to display data.) Do agency CEOs want to delegate dashboard access to their VPs and directors? (Determines whether to actively configure delegation in Phase 2.) Are there shared-services teams? Where should they appear — in an agency's "Your group," or only in the "Your company" view? Do you need custom analytical filters (Department, Location, Job Function)? (Determines whether to enrich with CSV org data.) Will dedicated analysts per agency run custom queries? (Determines whether to enable partitions in Phase 4.) Are any shared executives, cross-agency managers, global-partition analysts, or analyst ...

heading: A.2 Audit Manager Hierarchy per Agency
content: # Requires Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "User.Read.All" # Get all users and their managers — export to CSV for review Get-MgUser -All -Property Id,DisplayName,UserPrincipalName,Department,Manager ` -ExpandProperty Manager | Select-Object DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, Department, @{N='ManagerUPN';E={$_.Manager.AdditionalProperties.userPrincipalName}}, @{N='ManagerName';E={$_.Manager.AdditionalProperties.displayName}} | Export-Csv -Path "HierarchyAudit.csv" -NoTypeInformation # Find users with no manager set (potential tree breaks) Get-MgUser -All -Property DisplayName,UserPrincipalName,Manager ` -ExpandProperty Manager | Where-Object { $null -eq $_.Manager } | Select-Object DisplayName, UserPrincipalName | Format-Table -AutoSize

heading: 1. Modular Flow — 3 Prompts
content: How the Flow Works Run these three prompts in order , attaching the same org data file to each. Each prompt produces a self-contained report you can save. The prompts guide you to the next step automatically. Prompt 1: Data Readiness    Prompt 2: Dashboard Impact    Prompt 3: Remediation Plan First time? Follow these steps: Open your AI tool — the Microsoft 365 Copilot Analyst Agent or Copilot Chat (recommended). Have the org data file (Excel or CSV) saved and ready on your computer. Find Prompt 1 below and click its Copy button, then paste it into the AI tool. In the same message, attach the org data file , then send it. Read the report it produces and save it (for example, copy it into a Word or text document). Repeat steps 3–5 for Prompt 2 , then Prompt 3 — attaching the same file each time. Fix the file using the reports' guidance, then run Prompt 1 again to confirm it passes before uploading.

heading: Prompt 1 of 3 — Data Readiness Assessment
content: Purpose: Is the file structurally ready for upload? Checks column names, data types, hierarchy integrity, and data quality. Copy # ROLE You are a Microsoft Viva Insights Organizational Data Validation Specialist . Analyse the attached org data file and produce a DATA READINESS REPORT. # TASK Validate this org data file for upload readiness to the Copilot Dashboard / Viva Insights platform. # FILE SCHEMA AND TENANT-STATE GATE - If columns start with "Microsoft_" → Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 schema - If columns use PersonId/ManagerId/Organization → Viva Insights organizational-data schema - If NEITHER matches → CRITICAL: non-standard column names. Produce a rename mapping. - Header detection identifies schema only. It does NOT prove which add/edit portal is enabled for the tenant. - The operational path is controlled by the tenant's latest Message Center migration notice and the actual add/edit controls in Microsoft 365 admin center and Viva Insights. If this evidence is not supplied, report "Tenant upload path: UNCONFIRMED" and do not direct the user to either portal. - Microsoft sources conflict: https://learn.microsoft.com/viva/insights/advanced/admin/manage-settings-...

heading: 3. Usage Scenarios
content: Scenario 1: Pre-Upload Validation A customer sends you their org data CSV before uploading to Viva Insights. Paste the prompt + attach the file. The AI produces a validation report with fixes to apply before upload. Scenario 2: Dashboard Filter Planning A customer has multiple hierarchy columns (Groups, Branch, Section, Org Level) and wants help deciding what to map to Organization vs FunctionType vs custom attributes. The prompt's filter mapping section handles this. Scenario 3: Post-Upload Troubleshooting The Copilot Dashboard shows "--" for most groups, or the Organization filter is missing. Paste the prompt + attach the file they uploaded. The AI identifies what went wrong (e.g., empty Organization column, broken manager hierarchy). Scenario 4: Multi-Agency Data Review A government or multi-agency customer has complex hierarchy with agencies, divisions, branches, and sections. The prompt helps decide the right mapping for isolation vs default-priority architecture.

heading: 3. Quick Decision Checklist
content: Define whether the question is about billing, consumption, adoption, impact, value, or audit . Define the required population: licensed Copilot users, unlicensed Copilot Chat users, a partition, a manager group, or all covered events. Confirm the least-privileged role or API permission and the supported cloud environment. Record the source, scope, period, refresh timestamp, identifiers, and privacy treatment with every output. Reconcile against the authoritative surface: billing portal for charges, source report for adoption, or Purview for audit events. Do not combine sources until metric definitions, time boundaries, and identity handling are explicitly mapped.
